inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. B 257 of 1938.)
PANDIḶḶAPALLE, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Salb set up in front of the Venkaṭēśvarasvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1476, Ānanda, Phālguṇa śu. 15, corresponding to March 8,
Friday, 1555 A.D. (current).

Damaged and registers the gift of lands made by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kōnaya
Dēva mahārāja, son of Gobbūri Timmarāyajadēva mahārāja to several Brahmins and
to the temples of Perumāḷu. Vināyaka, Channarāya, Mailaradēva, Aṁkhāladēvi at
Pandiḷḷapalli, and also to the artisans who participated in the construction of these
temples. The village Paṁdiḷḷapalli is stated to have been given as amaram to the
donor by Nandēla Timmarāja, situated in the Ganṭikōṭa. It also refers to the grant
of the taxes like perusuṅkam grāmakaṭnam for maintaining the dīpārādhana.

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. B 289 of 1938.)
UPPALŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the front of Chanḍēśvarī temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1475, Pramādīcha, Māgha ba. 11, Corresponding to January 29,
Monday, 1554 A.D.

Registers the gift of Vipravinōdi paṁnu (tax collected from Vipravinōdins)
by Sāḷuva Gōvindayya, son of Vipravinōdi Pedda Gōvindayya of Kāśyapa gōtra,
Āpastaṁba sūtra and yajus-sākha and China Gōparāju, son of Ma[la*]yālapu Gōparāju,
for maintaining the worship and perpetual lamp to the god Mādhavēśvara at
Vuppalūru alias Dēvarāja samudramu in conjunction of the mahājanas of Vuppalūru
for the merit of the members of their community and other stationed at different
places (Kēśavayya of Chenūru, Parvvatayya of Chāṁgala maṛi; Padmābhatta Vallabhayya
stationed in the Turushakarājya; Gajavipravenōdi Kēśavayya stationed at Vōḍerajya
i.e., Ōḍarājya and others).

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. B 271 of 1936.)
IÑJĒḌU, KOILKUNTLA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up against the prākāra wall (inside), to the proper right of the main
entrance into the temple of Veṅkaṭēśvara.

It is dated in Śaka 1476, Ānanda, Chaitra śu. 10, Corresponding to March 13,
Tuesday, 1554 A.D.

Registers the gift of income of 0113 ka per year by Parvvatayya, Anantamayya,
sons of Vallabhayya of Kāsyapa gōtra, Kēśavayya of Vāsishṭha gōtra with the
permission of a body of Vipravinōdins consisting of different gōtras, sūtras with
different nomenclatures to the god Veṅkaṭanādha of Iñjēḍu on the occasion of Śrī
Rāmajayanthi, for the properity of their community. The said gift is granted to
them by Aśēshamahājanas of Iñjēḍu.

l.te: duveṁccēsi vunna śrīveṁkaṭanādhuni divyaśrī pāda padmaṁbu-
taṁ padaṁ || maṁgaḷamahā śrī śrī śrī jē·i (yu)nu ||

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. B 255 of 1938.)
PANDIḶḶAPALLI, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Venkaṭēśvarasvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1476, Ānanda, Phālguṇa śu. 15, corresponding to March 8,
Friday, 1555 A.D. (current).

It registers the gift of lands (details specified below) for the offerings to gods
and also for maintaining various temple officials in the temple of Prasannarāya at
Panḍiḷḷapalli by Gobbūri Timmayadēvamahārāja. The gifts of lands are as follows:
10 Kha of lands for the offerings (āragiṁpulu) 3 Kha for Tiruvaliku, 2 kha for
Chatupaḍi (offering of Sandal) and 10 kha for Aruvenkaṭādri, 4 kha for Viśvakshēṇa
of Sambaṭūru 3 kha for Bhōjanampalli Śrīnivāsaya, 4 kha for Kasidōju Budōju of
Venkatarāyani Kōvilagamti (barber?), 1 kha for dancing lady (dāsari sāni), 4 kha for
Ḍōla Nāgasarālavāru, who perform the music thrice in a day and 1 kha for Bayana,
the carpenter of the temple who prepared the wooden beams and clamps for the
temple building.

l.te: na kāśa sidōju budōjugāriki peṭina cē-
nu ê 4 nu dāsarisāniki peṭina cēnu ê 1 nnu
guḍiki dūlālu daṁtelu cekkina bayanaku cē-

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. B 243 of 1938.)
KŌḌŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the temple of Chennakēśava svāmi.

It is dated in Śaka 1478, Naḷa, Pushya ba. 12, Corresponding to December 28,
Monday, 1556 A.D.

It registers the gift of land by Chennaya, the son of Timmaya to the god
Chennakēśavamūrthi of AṁdirājuKōḍūru situated in Gaṇḍikōta-sīma. Gaṇdikōta-
sīma, where the gift village situated was given as amaranāyaṁkara by the king to
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Nandēla Timmayadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. B 296 of 1938.)
VELUDURTI, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A slab set up in front of the Chennakēśvarasvāmi temple.

This record is dated Śaka 1479, Naḷa, Phālguṇa śu. 15, Corresponding to February
13, Saturday, 1557 A.D. (current).

It states that Vōbaḷarāju, son of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rēmaṭikoṇḍa Vobularāju of
Sōma-vaṁśa and Ātrēyagōtra installed an image of Ēgu-Perumāḷu. He bought this
image for 12 varahās and performing offermed and worship. He had also constructed
the prakāra wall and a flower garden to the temple of Chennarāya, Yēgu (yajna)
Perumāl and Lakshmi-dēvamma.

l.te: nna[kēśava] perumāḷaku yeggu perumāḷanu paṁḍaiṁḍu varahalu
[śā]va kānike sēsi sama¨rpiṁci tecci pratiṣṭha sēyiṁcenu [||*] dēvu[ni*]

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. B 276 of 1938.)
SAMBAṬŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Chennakēśvasvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1479, Pingaḷa, Āsvija śu. 2, Corresponding to September 24th,
Friday, 1557 A.D.

It records the sale deed of a land situated near Komāḷḷa kālva (canal) and
Battula kālva, of sowing capacity of 13 puṭṭis, one kuncha and two kumṭas for 50
varahas at Cheḍipirāla for maintaining the offerings in different months to the god
Kēśavaperumal at Sambaṭūru alias Śrī Bhāshyapuram, by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Nandēḷa
China Ahōbalēśvaradēva mahārāju. The distribution of the gift is as follows 22
varāhas of land for maintaining the offerings in the month of Āśvija, 10 varāhas for
the offerings (āragimpu) in the month of Chaitra, 10 varāhas for the offerings in the
month of Vaiśākha and 3 varāhas for performing the tiruhāram in the maṇḍapa of
the temple of Kēśava perumal. It is stated that the gift of one-fourth of the prasāda
due to the donee from the temple, should be enjoyed by Bhōjanapalle
Kṛishṅamāchāryyalu for a long time. Chaḍipirāla is stated to belong to Mulkināḍu-
sīma, in Ghaṇḍikota-sima, of the Udayagiri-chāvaḍi, the Mulkināḍu-sīma has been
granted as nāyaṁkara by the king.

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. B 215 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up on the road side facing the shrine of Ankālamma.

It is dated Śaka 1480, (wrong for 1479) Pingaḷa, Āsvija śu. 3, Corresponding to
September 25, Saturday, 1557 A.D.

Registers a gift of land measuring one kha three na, three puṭṭi and a Mutta,
situated between the two canals at Chadpurēla by China Avubhalarāju, the son of
China Avibaḷarāju and the grandson of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Nandyīla Peda Avubhalarāja
of Ātrēya gōtra, Yajuśśākha and Āpastaṁbha sutra, for maintaining the tirunāḷu in
the months of Āśviyuja, Vayiśākha, Jyēshṭha, to the god Chaṁnarāya at
Śrī Bhāshyapuram. The inscription refers to the numerical number 10 (rūkas ?)
probably a portion of the income derived from the above gift for the maintenance
of festivals (Tirumālu).

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. B 195 of 1938.)
ANIMELA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the image of Gaṇēśa to the west of the village Animela.

This record is dated Śaka 1479, Pingaḷa, Kārttika śu. 11, corresponding to
November 2, Tuesday, 1557 A.D.

Records the remission of taxes like Kānika, Kaṭnam, āsuvechham, dommari-pannu,
sidhāyam, on barbers (mangaḷa-vōjulu) of Animela by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāju
Tirumalrāju Ayyadēva mahārāju, while Sadāśivadēvarāya was ruling the country.

l.te: vvaru tappinānu tamatalli taṁḍṟlanu vāraṇāśini • • • kalu kō-
śina pāpānabōduru gaṁggāvatilōnu gaṁgōvunu caṁpina pāpā-

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. B 270 of 1936.)
INJĒḌU, SIRUVEL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up against the prākāra wall (inside), to the proper right of the main
entrance into the temple of Veṅkaṭēśvara.

It is dated Śaka 1480, Kāḷayukti, Chaitra ba. 6, corresponding to April 9,
Saturday, 1558 A.D. f.d.t. °06.

Slightly damaged. Records the remission of taxes like pannu, Kānika, mangali
pannu (taxes on barbers), Dommari-pannu, Veṭivēmulu etc., collected from the villagers
of Gomdalūru sīma by Konḍrājayyadēva mahārājulu, son of the mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Rāmarāja Veṅkatēśa. The Gomdalūrisīma is said to have been given as nāyaṁkara
by the king to Rāmarāju Veṅkaṭēśa.

edition.h4: TEXT. A crescent, the Vaishna cast mark and the Sun are carved at the begining

l.te: kānika kaṭṇālu doṁmmari paṁnnu veṭivēmulu sa-

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. B 276 of 1936.)
TUMMALAPEṆṬA, KOYILAKUNTLA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A mutilated slab lying in front of the Āñjanēya temple.

This inscription is dated in Śaka [1480], Pingaḷa, Phālguṇa, śu. 11, corresponding to
February 28, Monday, 1558 A.D.

Registers the remission of certain taxes like kāparala paṁnnu, Kāni[ke*] Dommari
paṁnu, Siddhāyamu and Veṭivēmulu to the barbers (mangaḷavōjulu) situated in
Tummalapeṇṭa village in Avukasīma as in the case of the gift given to Komḍoju by
Sadāśivadēva mahārāya, by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja Tirumalarājayya dēvamahārāja
to Timmoju.

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. B 258 of 1936.)
KĀNĀLA, NANDYALA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab lying in front of the temple of Chennakēśavasvāmi.

It is dated Śaka 1481, Siddhārthi, Pushya, śu. 1, Makarasaṁkrānti, corresponding
to December 29, Friday, 1559 A.D.

Registers the gift of ten varāhās derived from the taxes of the village Pedda
Krānāla alias Krishṇarāya Samudramu agrāhāra belongs to Telugurāya for maintaining
the perpetual lamps and door way lamps in the temple of Chennakēśavara-Perumālu
by Kṛishṇamarājayyadēva mahārāju. Kṛishṇamārājayyadēva is described as the son of
Mūrthirājayya dēva mahārāja and the grandson of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Āravīṭi
Bukkarāju Rāmarāju. The gift village is stated to have been situated in the Śiruvōla-
sīma, which was given as nāyaṁkara to the donor Kṛishṇarājayya dēva mahārāja by
the king.

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. B 289 of 1936.)
KUṀKANŪRU, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the well called Mādireḍḍibhāvi to the west of the village.
It is dated Śaka 148[4], Sidhārtha, Bhādrapada, śu. 11, Irregular.

It records the conversion of a gift well into a dharma ētāmu by the sons of
Kaṁkanūri Vobayya of Aśvalāyanasūtra and Gautama gōtra (probably for use of
public) and also registers the gift of land in the cyclic year Naḷa probably for
maintaining the dharmaētāmu for the merit of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Pina Śrī Raṁgarāju.
The gift village is stated to have been situated in the nāyaṁkara of Tēkaṁṭikōṭa.
The Tēkaṁṭikōṭa is stated to have been given as amaranāyaṁkara to Timmarāju,
the son of Pinaśrī Raṁgarāju by Sadāśivadēvamahārāya.

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. B 265 of 1938.)
PEADDANAPĀḌU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up on the north side of the Śiva temple.

It is dated Śaka 1484, Duṁdhubhi, [Phālguna], śu. 5, corresponding to November
1, Sunday, 1562 A.D.

Damaged and incomplete. It seems to register the gift of the village Peddanapāḍu
located in Avuku-sīma by certain Aṁnnappanāyaka. It also mentions Raghunātharājaya
dēvamahārāju, son of • • • • • mala rājayyadēva mahārāju.

l.te: bulu 1484 ·agunēṭi duṁdubhi saṁvatsara phālguṇa śu[5]luṁ śrī

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. B 277 of 1938.)
SAMBAṬŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab lying outside Chennakēśava temple.

This undated record can be assigned to the period of Sadāśivadēvarāya both on
palaeographical and internal evidences.

Registers the gift of land costing 5 varahas for maintaining the annual boat
festivals, 10 varahas of land for maintaining the annual Tirunāḷḷu festival to the god
Kēśava Perumāl at Sambaṭūru otherwise known as Śrī Bhāshyapuram by
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara China Ōbulayyadēva mahārāja, son of Nandēla Ōbulayyadēva-
mahārāja. It further records that the Chief offered gift of the prasādam due to him
by the temple to Bhōjanampalli Krishṇayyamgaru. The village Chaḍipirala situated in
Mulkināṭi-sima of Ghaṇḍikōṭa sīma which is a part of the main terrtorial division
Udayagiri chāvaḍi which has been granted to the donor as a nāyaṁkara by the king.

l.te: nu trivācikaṁgānu sama¨rpiṁstimi [||*] yi reṁḍu ti-

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. B 322 of 1936.)
PĀLAGIRI, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up on the steps in front of the Chennakēśava temple.

This undated inscription can be assigned to the period of Sadāśivadēvarāya on
the basis of internal evidence.

Mutilated and damaged. Registers the gifts of lands at • • • • • Dēvayapalle probably
to the temple of Chennakēśvarāya.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. B 218 of 1938.)
CHAḌĪPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Vēṇugōpālasvāmin temple.

It is dated Śaka 1501, (wrong for 1500) Bahudhānya, Āshāḍha, śu. 12, corresponding
to Monday, June 1, 1578 A.D.

It states that while Śrī Raṅgarāyadēva mahārāya was ruling over the country
from Penugoṇḍa, his subordianate Kaṭṭamāna Singarrājayya of Kāśyapa gōtra, Āpastamba
sūtra, Yujuśākha and grandson of Śrngāra mahāpatra and the son of (his daughter)
Baiyyama of Ātrēyagōtra of Sōmavamsa granted the gift of the income derived
from the product of lands in different places situated in Chadupurēla in Ghaṇḍikoṭa
which was given as nāyaṁkara by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Nandyāla Nārasimhaparājayyadēva
mahārāja to the donor. It is further stated that the gift is given in the occasion of
renovating the silted up channels for the benefit of the temples Śrī Agastyēśvara and
Gōpālakrisṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. B 313 of 1938.)
GŌRANṬLA, KURNOOL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Mādhavasvāmi temple.

It is dated Śaka 1507, Pārthiva, Māgha, śu. 7, corresponding to January 17,
Monday 1586 A.D. (current).

It records the gift of the village Gōranṭla situated to Dōni Veludurti sīma by
Rangapparājayyadēvamahārāja of Āravīṭi family together with kāḍāraṁbha, nīrāraṁbha
and other incomes, for maintaining the services in the temple of Mādhavarāya of the
place. The donor is described as the son of Veṅkaṭādrirāja and the grandson of
Śrīraṅgarāja belonging to Ātrēya gōtra, Āpastambha sūtra, Yajuśākha of Sōmavaṁsa.
The donor is described as the ruler of Dōniveludurti-sīma.

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. B 198 of 1938.)
ANIMELA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Sangamēśvara temple.

It is dated Śaka 1514, Nandana, Adhika-Āshāḍha, śu. 15, lunar eclipse, corresponding
to June 14, Wednesday 1592, A.D.

It records the gift of lands by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gangādharrāju Dēvamahārāju,
son of Guruvarāja of the Kāśyapgōtra, Āpastambasūtra, Yajuśākha and Sūryavaṁsa
for maintaining the worship and food offerings to the god Sangamēśvara. The gift is
said to have been given on the banks of the river Aghanāsini Pāvani, i.e., Penna
river. The gift is given by the donor while the king Venkaṭapatirāya was ruling over
the country from Penugonḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. B 211 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the Pārvati temple, in the Agastyēśvarasvāmi temple complex.
It is dated Śaka 1515, Khara, Chaitra śu. 15, Friday. Irregular.

It registers the gift of a few villages namely Chaḍipirāla, Yarraguḍipāḍu and
other two villages by four individuals Nāgireḍḍi, son of Kūtāri Veṅkatayya, Nalayallaya,
son of Pāpa Timmaya, Koṇḍaya, son of Balabhadraya and Veṅkaṭayya, son of
Vanam Tirumalayya for the annual festivals (Tiruṇālu) in the month of Jyēshṭha.
Jointly (ēkastulai) while rājādhirāja, rājaparamēśvara Śrī Viraprātāpa Śrī Veṁkaṭarāya
(Venkaṭapati I) was ruling the country from the capital Penugoṇḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. B 325 of 1938.)
PRĀTAKŌṬA, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A broken slab set up in front of the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple.
It is dated Śaka 1522, Parīdhāvi, Māgha śu. 15, Irregular.

It registers the gift of lands (?) for maintaining the offerings and perpetual lamps
in the temples of Nāgēśvaradēva, Vīrabhadra, Gaṇādhipati, Mayilāradēva and Vaishṇava
temples like Chaṁnakēśava perumāḷ, Hanumaṁtha, etc., at Prātakōṭa by Nārayaṇayya,
son of Purāṇāṁ Bhūpayya. The gift is said to have been given for the merit of
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Rāmarāja Gōpālayyarājayyadēva, during the reigning period of
Veṁkaṭapatidēvarāya.

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. B 271 of 1938.)
SAMBAṬŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a broken slab lying in front of the Chennakēśava temple.

It is dated Śaka 1527, Viśvāvasu, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15, Friday corresponding to July 20,
(Saturday) 1605, A.D.

Registers the court agreement on the cultivation of land made by Duggarāju
Nārapa, the mudrakartta of Raghunāthanāyaka on the orders of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Jillēḷḷa Chinna Rangapati rāja mahārāju to the kāmpus of Kundūra on stipulation to
supply 23 kha of product and also Kānika of the given land for ninty seven years
continously. It is further record is stated to have been issued during the period of
Śrī Vīra Venkaṭapatidēva mahārāju who was ruling the country from Penugoṇḍa.

l.te: vasu saṁvatsara śrāvaṇa śu 15 śu śrīmadrājādhirājarā-
ṁgānu yēṁḍu 1 ki ê 1 ka | • 2[3] guttānu | • ni ¨r •

l.note: Numerical number 9 is engraved, followed by the word Yēḍu which means ninty seven years.

laku tappinavāru tamatalli taṁḍrula vāraṇāśi lō va-

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. B 286 of 1938.)
UPPALŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the Ānjanēya temple.
It is dated Śaka 1592, Naḷa (wrong for Sādhāraṇa), Bhādrapada, śu. 15, Irregular.

Fragmentary and incomplete. Mentions the king rājādhirāja, rājaparamēśvara
Śrī Vīravenkaṭadēva mahārāya.

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. B 241 of 1936.)
VANIKUNṬA VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the tank about 15 k.m., to the west of the village.

It is dated Śaka 1619, Bahudhānya, Āsvīja, Śu. 10. The Cyclic year Bahudhānya
corresponds to the Śaka 1620 and the date of the record may be corresponded to
October 4, Tuesday, 1698 A.D.

It records the gift of the village Vaniguṁṭa situated in the Vinikoṇḍasīma with
let and hindrance by Veṁkaṭappanāyaka, the son of Rāyapanāyaka and the grandson
of Veligōṭi Pedakoṁḍamanāyaka of Rēcharla gōtra to Aṁ[ṇa]amgarāchārya, son of
Śrī Raṁggachāryya and the grandson of Kaṁḍāḷa Bhāvanāchārya. The donor is
stated to have received the Vinikoṇḍa sīma as nāyaṁkara from the king.

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. B 311 of 1936.)
INDUKŪRU KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up at the entarance of the village (back side of the
inscription No. 310 of 1938.)
This inscription is dated in the cyclic year Plava, Mārgaśira śu. 10, Tuesday.

States that Timmanna, the mudrakarta of Chinabhamḍāram Nāgappanāyaka
leased out the waste land (bīḍu) to the Kāṁpus of Yimdupura from the cyclic year
vyaya and they agreed to pay remuneration at the specified rate continuously from
the third year of agreement for about eight years. Nāgappanāyaka is described as the
officer of the treasury (Bokkasaṁ-bhaṁḍḍara-kartta) of the king.

l.note: The wordgajam is engraved at the end of the inscription in late characters.

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. B 210 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west prākāra wall of the Agastyēśvara temple.

It is dated in Śaka 147(1), Sādhāraṇa, Chaitra, śu. 5, corresponding to March 24,
1549 A.D.
Fragmentary. It records the date portion and incompleted title śrīmadrājadhirā[ja*]

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. B 310 of 1938.)
MAILAVARAM, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
A Fragment of a slab containing the images of the Saptamāṭṛkas, fixed into the
South wall of the Śiva temple.

This undated record can be assigned to 16th Century A.D. on palaeographical
grounds.

Incomplete. Refers to the royal titles such as Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara. Harirāyavibhāla,
Mūruurāyaragaṇḍa, etc.,

pū¨rvadakṣiṇa paścima samu

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. B 255 of 1938.)
PANDIḶḶAPALLE KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab setup in front of the Veṇkṭeśvarasvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1476, Ānanda, Phālguṇa, śu. 15, (corresponding to March 8,
Friday (expired) 1554 A.D.

It registers the gift of lands to the temple of Prasanna Venkaṭeśvarāya and to
other Gods by Gōbūri Timmayadēvamahārāja for the merit of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Nandyāla Tiṁmayarājayadēvamahārāja and also to several individuals (names mentioned)
for the service rendered to the temple.

l.te: nu ê 4 nu dāsarisāniki peṭina cēnu ê 1 nnu

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. B 234 of 1938.)
GANGIREḌḌIPALLE (Hamlet of Animela) KAMALAPURAM TALUK,
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Boulder in a field about 2 kilometres to the south of the village.

It is dated in Śaka 1509, Sarvajit, Pushya, ba. 30, corresponding to January 17,
Wednesday, 1587 A.D. (current).

Damaged and incomplete. Seems to register the gift of land of one paṁdumu by
Maṭli Kumāra Anantharājadēvachōḍa Mahārāju probably to the son (name not
mentioned) of certain Māchi Ayya who was maintaining this gift.

l.te: sa¨rpajitu saṁvatsara puṣyaba 30 lu śrīmaṁnmahāmaṁḍa[lēśvara]maṭli kumāra ·a-

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. B 280 of 1938.)
SAMBAṬŪRU KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of a shrine on the north side of the Chennakēśava temple.

It is dated in the cyclic year Rudhirōdgāri, Kārthīka, śu. 10, Since the reigning
period of this chief falls in between, 1588 A.D. and 1602 A.D. the given year
correspondings to October 26, Tuesday, A.D.

It states that Raghunāthayya, the kāryakarta of the king has donated the waste
land (biḍu) for cultivation to the kāmpus of Animela at the rate of 92 guttas per
acre for about seven years continuosly. It also records the remission of taxes called
kānike, asavechham (dasavanda ?) and davasāyi to the government for these seven
years.

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. B 278 of 1938.)
SAMBAṬŪRU KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a fragmentary some lying outside the prākāra on the west side of the
Chennakēśava temple.

This record is dated in cyclic year Rudhirōdgāri, Kārttīka, śu. 10. On the basis
of palaecgraphy and internal evidence it can be assigned to 16th century A.D.

This incomplete record mention the king mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Maṭli Kumāra
Anantharājayyadēva Chōḍa mahārāja and his Kāryakarna.

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. B 108 of 1937-38.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT, TAMIL NADU.
inscription.p:
On the west wall of the Garuḍamaṇḍapa, V Prākāra in the temple of śrī
Ranganathasvāmi.

It is dated in Śaka 1593, Virōdhakṛit, Māgha ba. 3, Monday, Uttiranakshatram
regularly corresponding Feruary 5, Monday, 1672 A.D.,

Registers a gift of land in the village Kūttappāra in Kōnāḍu to the east of
Triruchināpalli by Basavappa-Nāyuḍu, son of Jaṅgama-Nāyuḍu, of Kāsyapa gōtra for
offerings and worship to the god on the 8th day of the festival instituted by
Chokkanāthanāyaka when the god was taken in procession to Vasanthavilāsa maṇḍapam
in Nāchhiyār-tōppu.
(Published in South Indaia Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 548, p 529-30).

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. B 103 of 1938.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Garuḍamaṇḍapa in the temple of Śrī Ranganathasvāmi.

It is dated in Śaka 1593, Virōdhikṛit, Phālguna, śu. 13, Friday, Makhā corresponding
to March 1, Friday 1672 A.D.

Records the gift of land to the temple by Chōḍa Aḷagāri, the pradhāni and the
son of Kāpanāyaka and the grandson of Chōḍi-Aḷagādrināyaka on behalf
Viśvanathanāyaka Chokkanāthanāyaka.

edition.p: (Published in South Indaia Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 549, p 531-32.)

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. B 102 of 1938.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the Garuḍamaṇḍapa in the V Prākāra of the
Śrī Ranganātha temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1596, Ānanda, Śravaṇa, ba. 5, Tuesday, (Bhaumavāsara)
Aśvani nakshtra corresponding to August 11, Tuesday 1674 A.D. f.d.t. 77.

It registers a gift of the village Vūkkakuḍi situated in Tiruvālūruvanita in Chōḷa-
dēśa and also yielding 600 rēkhagaduyāna for maintaining the food offering daily
(the quantity of each raw food items mentioned) to the god Ashṭbhuja Gōpalakṛishṇa
consecrated on the northen side of the Viṭṭhalēśvara shrine in the temple complex
of śrī Raṇganātha at Tiruvaraṅgam by Chinna Bommanāyaka and the same deposited
in the śribhaṇḍāramu of the temple.
(Published in South Indaia Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 550, p. 532.)

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. B 104 of 1938.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the Garuḍamaṇḍapa in the fifth Prākāra of the
śrī Ranganātha temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1596, Ānanda, Śravana, ba. 5, Tuesday, Aśvanī, corresponding
to August 11, Tuesday 1674 A.D.

Records the gift of the villages Taḍām and Ūkkaguḍi in Tiruvārūru-vaṇita in
Chōla-dēśa by Chinna Bommināyaka in Madura of the kingdom of Viśvanātha
nāyaka Chokkanātha nāyaka, for offering to the god Raṅganātha when deity halted
before shrine of Gōpālakṛishṇa.
(Published in South Indaia Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 551, pp. 533-34.)

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. B 1 of 1937.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the slab built into the pavement on the east side of the first prākāra in the
Ranganāthasvāmi temple.

This is dated in the cyclic year Vishnu (vṛishu ?), śrāvana, śu. (10), Sunday,
palaeographically this record can be assigned to 18th century A.D. The date of the
record, probably is corresponding to August 3, Sunday 1701 A.D. f.d.t. 35.

States that the inscription is destroyed and got re-engraved, originaly donated by
the king Chokkanātha-Nāyaka and his Queen Maṅgammavāru and kept on the east
side of the tiru-vaṇṇāḻigai at the instance of the servants of the temple.
(Published in South Indaia Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 569, pp. 549.)

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. B 2 of 1937.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Engraved on the another slab (III) built into the pavement on the east of the
first prākāra in the temple of Śrī Ranganāthasvāmi.

This undated record can be assigned to 18th century on internal and palaeographical
evidences.

Refers to the prayers by Mangammavāru, Muddu Chandrasēkhammavāru
Kamalājammavāru, Jānakammavāru and Induvadanammavāru, the queens of
Chokkanāthanāyaka for the disapparance of his Chāya i.e. probably refers to the act
of prayers at the time of his death.

edition.p: (Published in South Indaia Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 563, pp. 536-37.)

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. B 4 of 1937.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the slab built into the pavement on the east side of the first prākāra in the
Ranganāthasvāmi temple.

It is dated in the cyclic year Vibhava, Chaitra, babula and on the basis of
internal evidence this can be assigned to 1688 A.D.

Refers to the donation of Kirīṭa to the god Śrī Ranganāyaka by Muddamma
dēvulu, the queen of Śrīrangakṛishṇa muddu Vīrappanāyaka.
(Published in South Indaia Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 558, p. 540.)

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. B 3 of 1937.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT, TAMIL NADU.
inscription.p:
On the slab (No. III) built into the pavement on the east side of the first
prākāra in the temple of Ranganāthasvāmi.

It is dated in the cyclic year Prabhava (worng for Vibhava), Āshāḍha, śu. 5,
Friday, corresponding to June 22, Friday, 1688 A.D.

Records the gift of two villages Iśanakūru and Nāṇakūru to one Śrīnivāsayya to
meet the expenses of worship and sahasranāmārchana and yamunārchakam and also
kīrīṭa (?) daily to the god for the merit of Muddummagāru, Queen of Śrīranga-
kṛishṇa Mudduvīrappanāyaka.

edition.p:
(Published in South Indaia Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 557 and 558
pp. 539-540.)

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. B 106 of 1938.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the Chandana maṇḍapa in the 2nd prākāra of the
Śrī Ranganātha temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1613, Pramōdūta Bhādrapada. śu. 15, pūrṇami (lunar eclipse)
corresponding to September 8, 1690 A.D.

Records the restoration of tīrtham and other privileges like aruḻappāḍu and
recitation of Ṛigvēda in the temple to Kumāra Veṅkaṭa Varadāchārvva. son of
Varadāchāryya and grandson of Āchchi Śrī Ranganārāyanācharyya of Gārgya gōtra,
Aśvalāyana-sūtra and Rik-sākha by the king Śrīrangakrishṇa Muddu Vīrappa nāyaka
(belongs the family) of Viśvanāthanāyaka. It is stated that these privileges enjoyed
from the time of Uḍaiyavar (Rāmānujā) by the family had been lapsed for some
time due to the departure of Āchchi Śrī Ranganārāyanācharyya to the North and
stayed these for long time.
(Published in South Indaia Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 560, pp. 541-42.)

l.te: viṁnapaṁ śrībhāṣyaṁ viṁnapaṁ modalayina svataṁtra svā-
lu bahugāvuṁnnāru ganaka ·akkaḍiki veṁccēśi ·akkaḍanē konni di-
koni mēmudattaṁ cēśina tirumāḷighē 1 lō vāsaṁ cēsuko-

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. B 49 of 1938.)
JAMBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT, TAMIL NADU.
inscription.p: Slab built into the eastern parapet wall of the Jambu-tērtha tank.

It is dated in the cyclic year Plava, Vaiyāśī 5 corresponding to 5th May, 3
Friday, 1661 A.D.

It refers to the construction of the nāṭaka-śāla (auditorium for dramas) of
Vijayaranga Chokkanātha by Pāṭakam Vayimda(pa)ya, the son of Śikshaka
Veṅkaṭēśvaraya.
(c.f. No. B. 48 of 1936.)

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. B 48 of 1938.)
JAMBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
This inscription mentions the cyclic year Śōbhakṛit. The cyclic year Śōbhakṛit is
fallen in the year Śaka 1643 and corresponding year 1772 A.D.

A singer (pāṭakam) Vaidyappaya, the son of Venkatēśvara, an instructor (śikshaka)
of the nāṭaka-śāla of Vijayaranga Chokkanātha nāyaka caused the construction of
steps and manḍapa to temple (?) of Jambukēśvaram.

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. B 10 of 1937.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the beam of a maṇḍapa, in the north side of the third prākāra of the
temple of Śrī Ranganāthasvāmi.
This undated record can be assigned to 18th century A.D. on internal grounds.

Records the construction of maṇḍapa for the recitations of Vēdas to the god
Ranganāyaka by Śrī Vijayaraṅga Chokkanātha.

edition.p: (Published in South Indian Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 570, p. 550.)

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. B 42 of 1938.)
JAMBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Loose stone in the 3rd prākāra Jambukēśvara temple.

This inscription can ben assigned to 18th century on the basis of palaeography
and other evidence.

Records the perpetural devotion (sadāsēva) of the king Vijayaraṅga Chokkanātha
Nāyaka.

l.note: A legend is engraved in Nāgari characters-Multāna Āpākāle, on the right

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. B 226 of 1936.)
MATUKUMALLI, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up behind the image of Āñjanēya, situated in the back-side of the
village Karaṇam's house.

This is dated Śaka 1406, Krōdhi, Kārttīka, śu. 15, Friday lunar eclipse, corresponding
to Wednesday, November 3, 1484 A.D. (not Friday).

Damaged. It registers, the gift of the village Maṟakaṁballi (Read: Mutukumpali
in A.R.Ep. 1935-36, p31), in Vinikonda-sīma, by mahāpātra Ajamakhāna sāmanta-
mahāpātra on the occasion of the lunar eclipse. The eclipse actually occurred in the
previous month i.e. Āśvajuya (1484 A.D., October 4, Monday), Ajamakhān was
holding the posts like Purāparīksha (officer in charge of town) and Deobhāṇḍāru
parīksha (officer incharge of temples), during the period of Purushottama Gajapati.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. B 277 of 1938.)
UYYĀLAVĀḌA, KOILKUNTLA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of a slab lying in the Chennakēśavasvāmi temple.

It may be assigned to 15th century on the basis of palaeography and internal
evidence.

Fragmentary and damaged. Refers to consecration of [Ahobale] Īśvara, installation
of Jayastaṁbha by Narasiṁha (pā)truḍu the vūligam of mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. B 281 of 1936.)
VIPANAGANḌLA, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the well called Bommanabhāvi.

This is dated Śaka 1632 (wrong for 1623), Vṛisha, Mārgaśira, ba. 10, Friday,
corresponding to December 13, 1701 A.D. f.d.t. °78 (Saturday).

Registers a gift of garden (tōṁta) as Īnām to Bommireḍḍi, the grandson of
Āvula Bommi reḍḍi for digging the common drinking water well (dharma gōḍi
bhāvi) in the village Viṁpinagaṁdla situated in Kandan Vōlisīṁā. The Kandanavōlu
is stated to have been given as mokhāsa by Abdula Wahābu Sāheb to Musākhān
while mahārājādhirāja Sultān Pādusha was ruiling over from Vijayāpura (Bījāpūr).

mati tōṁṭa bāviki tū¨rpuna ¬1 ||| tōṁṭṭa yiccināru

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. B 360 of 1937.)
MUTTUKŪRU, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a rock about a kilometer to the south-east of the village.

It is dated in Śaka 1738, Dhātu, Māgha, śu. 15, Tuesday corresponding to 23rd
February, 1817 A.D. (current).
Records that the pond (dōna), dug by Suguṭūri Immaḍi Chikkarāvalu.

l.te: tusaṁ | mūgha śu 15 lu | ma | rā ||

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. B 355 of 1937.)
CHERUKUVĀRIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Base (Probably plinth) of the Kāśī Viśvēśvarasvāmi temple.

This inscription is dated in Śaka year 1738, Dhātu, Māgha, ba. 5, corresponding
to Febraury 6, Thursday 1817 A.D.

It states that the construction of a maṇḍapa and a stone gateway and also
donation of the utsavavigraha of the goddess Chaṇḍīśvara by Immaḍi Chikkarāyalu,
for the merit of his father Immaḍi Chikkarāyalu of Suguṭūri family.

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. B 226 of 1936.)
TSADUM, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Rock near the tank at Mālapalle, a hamlet about a mile to the south of the village.
This is dated in Śaka 1780, Kīlaka, Māgha śu. 6, Irregular.

Worn out, Refers to the construction of stone wall to the tank and donated gift
of land for maintaining the repairs to the tank by Suguṭūri Chikarāya Tamme
Gaunayyavāru. This inscription seem to the engraved by Ānekanṭṭe Vīra Gaunayyavāru.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. B 362 of 1937.)
NELLIMAṆḌA, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in survey No. 45 near the tank called Rāmayyakunṭa.
This inscription mentions the cyclic year Prabhava kārttīka 1.
Damaged. Mentions mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chikkarāya.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. B 356 of 1937.)
CHINTAPARTIVĀRIPALLE, (Hamlet of Kambhamvāripalli), PUNGANUR
TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Rock near the Nāga stones at the west end of the tank bund.
. • .
. • .
It is dated in cyclic year Dhātu, Āshāḍha, śu.

Badly damaged. Seems to register some gifts made by Immaḍi Chikkarāya of
the Sugutūri family. Mention Āvulapilli.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. B 241 of 1938.)
KŌḌŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Pillar set up at the entrance into the compound of the
Chennakēśava svāmi temple.

This archaic record is dated Śaka 878, (in words) uttarāyaṇa, corresponding 956
A.D.

Registers the gift of 8 marturus of land located in front of the temple of Bhaṭāra
by Bijja[ṇa], the rule of the village Gōḻūru to Gaṭṭyabōya, son of Kāṭaya raḍḍi. The
inscriptions is engraved by Vīrāchāri.

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. B 213 of 1936.)
INUMELLA, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in Survey No. 479 to the west of the village.
It is dated in Śaka 1035 corresponding to 1113 A.D.

It records the construction of temple (guḍi) by a certain Savuka-bōya situated on
the banks of the Guṇḍikaṟiti (i.e., Guṇḍlakamma). He is described as the grandson of
Trikaṭa-bāya, the raṭṭaḍi of the village Tagaṭūru and records the grant of land,
probably a puṭṭi in extent, to the same temple.

inscription.head:
No. 165A.
(A.R. No. B 243 of 1936.)
ĒLŪRPĀḌU, BHIMAVARAM TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On Nandi-pillar set up in front of the Bhīmēśvara temple.
It is dated in Śaka 1057 (nag-ēndriyakh-ēmdu) corresponding to 1135 A.D.

States that Bhujabalabhīma Kāṭamanāyaka exempted the Brahmins, the ascetics,
their women, cattle and other things belonging to them from tolls, that are to be
collected at Ēṅḍapi rēvu and further states that he set up a nandi-pillar probably
contains this inscription in front of the temple of Mahādēva on the bank of the
river.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. B 303 of 1937.)
BHAṬṬIPRŌLU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar of the mukha-maṇḍapa in the Viṭṭhaēśvara temple.

It is dated Śaka 1066, Māgha ba. 14, Tuesday. The cyclic year being the Raktāshi
and the details of the date correspond to 1144 A.D., January 23.

Incomplete. Records the gift of the perpetual lamp to the temple of Viṭṭīśvaradēva
of Bhaṭṭiprōlu by Gaḍivana Gōkana for the merit of his parents. The lamps were
entrusted to aṁgarika Tumuka and naṁgarika Aṁgarikarole with the stipulation to
supply one māna of ghee everyday.

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. B 344 of 1938.)
MĀDĀLA, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On pillar lying in the Sagarēśvara śvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1077, expressed in Chronagram: Muni-śaila-kh-ēndu. Solar
eclipse (month not mentioned) corresponding to 1155 A.D.

It records the gift of 55 sheep (goṟiyalu) on the day of Solar eclipse for
maintaining the perpetual lamp in the temple of Sakalēśvara at Māvuṁdala, by
Javāḍa Nāmana belonging to Yājñavalka and Kāśyaka gōtra for the merit of himself.
The gift is entrusted to Bhamanabōya, son of Yimdula Mānebōya.

In the Sanskrit portion. Nāma is described as great grandson of Karna of
Velanāṇti Gonka family.

l.te: mugā māvuṁdala śrī sakalēśvara mahādēvaraku ·akhaṁḍa va-

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. B 348 of 1938.)
MĀḌUGALA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragments of a hero-stone kept in the Kāḷēśvara temple.
This is dated Śaka [1]105 Krōdhi corresponding to 1183-84 A.D.

Fragmentary and incomplete. Refers to the death of certain Sōmarāju who
jumped (nuṁgu) in to the battle field at Vēlūru (Vēlpūru ?).

l.note: First Five lines are engraved on the

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. B 289 of 1937.)
ĀMUDALAPALLE, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a muttilated nandi-pillar lying in Survey No. 267/2.

It is dated Śaka 1157, Manmatha, Pushya, ba 14, Uttarāyaṇa Sankrānti, Wednesday,
corresponding to 1236 A.D. (expired) January, 9.

A portion of this inscription is badly damaged and mulilated. The second side
of the record mentions the gift of 5 cows for maintaining the perpetual lamp in the
temple of Sōmēśvara at Kommūru for the merit of his parents by certain Bētreḍḍi.
The gift is said to have been entrusted to certain Prōla Muddaḍi with a stipulation
to supply one mānika of ghee of the temple daily for maintaining the said gift.

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. B 248 of 1936.)
KUMĀRADĒVAM, NIDADAVOLE TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Venkaṭēśvarasvāmi temple.
It is dated Śaka 1159, Uttarāyaṇa-sankrānti, corresponding to 1236 A.D. December.

Registers the gift of 15 chinnas (coins) for maintainig the aradivvya
probably for half day in the temple of Ishṭhakāmīśvaramahādēva at Gajjaramu
by Polisēnaollasāni, the daughter of Ollupa Mallināyaka.

edition.h4: TEXT. The sun and ardhachandra are engraved on the top of the

l.te: svasti [||*] śakava¨rṣabulu 1159 dagu nēṁṭi ·uttarā[ya*]ṇa saṁkrāṁtti nāṇḍu ·ollupa mallinā-

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. B 252 of 1936.)
KUMĀRADĒVAM, NIDADAVOLE TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Venkaṭēśvarasvāmi temple.

This is dated Śaka 1160, (Viḷaṁbi), Kārtīka, Mahādvādasi, Bṛihaspativāra (Thursday)
corresponding to 1238 A.D. October, 21, f.d.t. °14.

Records an agreement (oḍam baṟuka s.a oḍaṁbaḍi) made by the merchant guilds
(nakaram) to offer tax (suṁkkamu) as a gift to the god Ishṭakāmeśvaradeva at
Gajjaram.

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. B 251 of 1936.)
KUMĀRADĒVAM, NIDADAVOLE TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On another pillar in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Venkaṭēśvarasvāmi temple.
It is dated Śaka 1162 corresponding to 1240 A.D.

Damaged and incomplete. Seems to register a gift of land measuring kha at
Aripalli, na. 10 under the irrigation of Jāyanacheṟuvu etc., by Aḍapa Mahādēvana-
Peggada, the son of Muchchirāja, for offerings to the god Ishṭakāmīsvara Mahādēva.

l.te: lōnaṁ na 10 pū • raḍḍi • • • • • guḍimeṭṭa po-

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. B 298 of 1937.)
BHAṬṬIPRŌLU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the kalyāṇamaṇḍapa in the Viṭṭhalēśvara temple.

Dated in Śaka 1165 Śōbhakṛit, 12, and records only the date portion corresponding
to 1243 A.D.
Incomplete and mentions date portion only.

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. B 300 of 1937.)
BHAṬṬIPRŌLU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On another pillar in the kalyāṇaṇḍapa of the Viṭṭhalēśvarasvāmi temple.
This inscription is dated in Śaka 1169, Saumya, Kaṭaka Sankrānti. Irregular.

This is partly defaced and remaining portion states that the Pekkaṁḍru and
others of Uppalī (village) made a gift probably, of an income uppu rēvu sunkam
collected from the Salt pans (yupaṛēvula) called for maintaining the offerings to the
god Mahādēva at Bhaṭṭiprōlu. It further states that certain Amarēśvara peggaḍa son
of Appana Peggaḍa engraved this record.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. B 345 of 1938.)
MĀDĀLA, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar lying in the Sagarēśvara Svāmi temple.
It is dated Śaka 1176, Uttarāyaṇasankrānti, corresponding to 1254 A.D.

Records the gift of 55 sheep (goṟiyalu) for maintaining the perpetual lamp in the
temple of Sakalēśvara mahādēva by Mārebōya for the merit of his parents Gōnajavrōli
Nannebōya and mother (name not mentioned).

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. B 297 of 1937.)
BHAṬṬIPRŌLU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the kalyānamaṇḍapa of the Viṭṭhalēśvarasvāmi temple.

It is dated Śaka 1182, Raudri, Pushya, ba. 2, Sunday Makara Sankrānti corresponding
to 1260 A.D. 21 December. The day being the Tuesday.

Registers the gift of fifty five sheep for maintaining the perpetual lamp in the
temple of Bhōgīśvaradēva at Bhaṭṭiprōlu by principle servant (Bhōgēśvara devaramūla
bhṛityuḍu) Śrī Karaṇam Īśāna Peggaḍa for the merit of his parents Panṭipeggaḍa
Appana Peggaḍa and mother (name not known). The gift is entrusted to Dēvana
bōya and Prōḷe bōya, the sons of Paḷe bōya with the stipulation to maintain the gift
lamp by supplying one mānika per day. The engraver of the record is Mallēśvarapeggaḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. B 292 of 1937.)
BHAṬṬIPRŌLU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the kalyāṇamaṇḍapa of the Viṭṭhalēśvarasvāmi temple.

This inscription is dated Śaka 1182, Raudri, Pushya, ba. 10, Makara-sankrānti.
Wednesday, corresponding to 1260 A.D. December 10.
Incomplete. It mentions the name of the god Bhōgīśvaradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. B 349 of 1938.)
VĒLPŪRU, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying in front of the Rāmalingēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1184, Pushya, śu. 15, Tuesday and corresponds to 1262 A.D.
December 27, f.d.t. °17.

Broken and incomplete. It records the gift of golden-incense-bell (Kanakamaya
dhūpaghaṁṭa) by Pōti-seṭṭi, the husband of Gaṇapaṁa of Vēnukulagōtra to the god.
He also got constructed the doorway (gaḍapa) to the temple and donate the offerings.
It also refers to one Prōlasani of the above mentioned gōtra, probably the mother of
Pōti-seṭṭi.

l.te: • • • veṁttayu • nu pemamu gaḍapa yettinanu pu-

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. B 347 of 1938.)
MĀDĀLA, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar lying in the temple of Sagarēśvarasvāmi.
It is dated Śaka 1184, Uttarāyaṇasankrānti, corresponding to 1263 A.D.

Registers the gift of three māḍas of pa[ṭa]mu (Polamu ?) and fifty sheep for
maintaining the perpetual lamp (akahaṇḍavatti-dīpa) in the temple of Sakalēśvara at
Māvuṁdala (Mādāla) by Ponikaṁṭi Dēvaya bhaḷumḍu. Dēvayabhaḷu is described as
the faithful servant (Ishṭa-bhṛityuḍu) of Mahāpradhāni Bētana preggaḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. B 254 of 1936.)
KUMĀRADĒVAM, NIDADAVOLE TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the mukhamaṇḍapa of proper right of the Venkaṭēśvarasvāmi temple.
It is dated in Śaka 1188, Sankrānti, corresponding to 1266 A.D.

Damaged. Registers the gift of land measuring one puṭṭi situated in the land
namely Kumari Kunṭa at Gajjaramu to the temples of Ishṭa Kāmēśvaradēva and
Rāmanāthadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. B 291 of 1937.)
BHAṬṬIPRŌLU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the Kalyaṇamaṇḍapa of the Viṭṭalēśvarasvāmin.

It is dated Śaka 1232, Sādhāraṇa Pushya, śu. 8, Wednesday, corresponding to 30,
December, 1310 A.D.
Incomplete. It stops with reference to Śrī Bhōgēśvara Śrīmahādēva of Bhaṭṭiprōlu.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. B 253 of 1936.)
KUMĀRADĒVAM, NIDADAVOLE TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Venkaṭēśvarasvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1244, (Duṁdubhi) Mārgaśira, ba. 15, Solar eclipse, corresponding
to 1322 A.D. December 9, Thursday.

Registers the setting up of six pillars (āru kaṁbhamulu) in the temple Ishṭa
Kāmīśvaradēva by Appana-Peggaḍa Annaṁgāru and also gift of land measuring one
pandumu as his son's vṛitti to the temple by him.

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. B 193 of 1938.)
ANIMELA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A broken slab set up near the house of the karaṅam.
It is dated Śaka 1277, Plavanga, Kārttika śu. 5, Friday.

Damaged. Seems to register the sale of land by certain Prōlajiyya probably at
Animela. It mentions vudida paḍagādaṇu.

l.note: ā wordsvastiśrī ramanuṇdu is engraved in 9th century characters.

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. B 281 of 1938.)
SAMBAṬŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.

It is dated Śaka 1319, Īśvara, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 6, Tuesday, corresponding to August 14,
1397 A.D.

It registers the sale of half of the shares of vṛittis of the temple of Mūlasthānadēva
to Kēkaṭam Chaunḍajiyya by Mallajiyyalu for 40 ṭaṅkas in the presence of
Bhairajiyya and his brother Siṅgajiyya. The donor Mallajiyya is described as the son
of Bramaka who is the daughter of Sōmajiyya.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. B 205 of 1936.)
BOGGARAM (NEAR MUPPĀḶḶA), VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar lying in the Guṟṟālamma temple.

This record contains two dates. The first is dated in Śaka 1323, Vṛisha Phālguṇa,
śu. 15, Monday, corresponding to 1401 A.D. Feruary 28. While the second is dated in
Śaka 1276 (wrong for Śaka 1259) Īśvara, Pushya, śu. 2, Thursday, corresponding to
1337 A.D. December 25, Thursday.

The first part of record refers to the assemblage of the trade-communities viz.,
paradēsi, chālumūla-vubhaya-nānādēśa-samaste pekkaṁḍru, eḍūnṭ siṁhālu, baniva jangali
billmu nibbaru and billagumāḍlu (billa-kumārlu ?) in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the
Śōmēśvara temple Nelikuduti i.e., Nelikuduru.

The second part of the inscription records the gift of the village Boggaramu as
samaya-mānya and also the gift of a horse and an umbrella to the goddess Guṟṟāla
Paramēśvari by these trade guilds. It also specifies the contributions by various
communities in which billa gumāllu (billa-kumālu) and (yēḍnūṇṭ i simhyālu) are
included, and also records the exemptions of the taxes payable and the articles
purchased or sold at this place.

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. B 354 of 1937.)
SIDDHĀNTAM, GUDIVADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar lying in front of the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1326, Tāraṇa, Māgha, śu. 10, Tuesday corresponding to 22
January, 1405 A.D.

It states that the pillars were caused to be made of the mukhāmaṇḍapa of the
temple of Nagarēsvaradēva at Koṁḍavīḍu by Rāvipūṁḍi Vīriseṭṭi of Sidhiseṭlagōtra
for the merit of his father Saraṇiseṭṭi and mother Mārasāni. The donor is described
as the lord of Penugoṇḍapura and Vaiśyakulōttama.

l.te: nēṁṭi tāraṇa saṁvvatsara māṣu śu 10 ma [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. B 228 of 1936.)
NĀYANIPĀLEM, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Image set up on the tank-bund about a mile to the west of the village.

This epigraph is dated in the cyclic year Vīrōdhikṛīt, Kārttīka, śu. 12, Friday.
This inscription may be assigned to the 13th century A.D. on the basis palaeography
of the date of the record may be corresponded to 1491, A.D. October, 14.

It records the consecration of the image of Prōturāju by Chinayallappa, son of
Gādēla Yallappa on the tank-bund of Yedluru. It also records that half a kumcha of
land was given to the Kāsa who carved the image. From the date portion, which
also refers to the subsquent day Saturday (marnāḍu sanivāramnāḍu) it appears that
the grant was made on Saturday while the consecration took place on the previous
day.

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. B 263 of 1938.)
PEDDANAPĀḌU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Śiva temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1357, Ānanda, Māgha ba. 14, Friday corresponding to January
13, Thursday f.d.t., °85, 1435, A.D.

Records the consecration of the image of Śrī Vīrabadradēva by Nāgārāju, the
son of Cheṁgūmṭṭa Mallamarāju, of Śaunaka gōtra and Yajuś-śakha.

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. B 242 of 1936.)
VĒLPŪRU, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Rāmaliṅgasvāmin temple.

This is dated Śaka 1363, Raudri, Pushya ba. 30, Sunday ardhōdaya corresponding
to 1441 A.D. January 23, Monday f.d.t. °09. There was a solar eclipse on this day,
which was however, not mentioned in the record.

It registers the gift of land of 3 kha in extent situated at Cheṁnnapalli to the
God Velūpūru Rāmayadēva by Velupuri Kēśaojjulu for the merit of himself and his
parents.

l.te: nnapalli reḍḍi pāṭini kha 3 kṣētraṁ thāra[vō]-

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. B 264 of 1936.)
PEDDACHINTAKUNṬA, SIRUVEL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A broken slab lying in the compoud of the Sāmbaśivēśvara temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1364, Durmati, Pushya, śu. 15, Monday, lunar eclipse. The lunar
eclipse falls on Wednesday. Hence the date of corresponds to 27, Wednesday, 1441,
A.D. f.d.t. °92.

Records the confirmation of renewal of gift of lands to the eight temples such
as one kha or vṛiṭṭi to the god Rāmayadēva, one kha or vṛiṭṭi to the god
Gōpināthadēva, probably one kha (kha 011) to Mayilaradēva, kha (kha 011) to
Vināyaka, kha 01 to goddess Chāvunḍēśvari, Gamgala vṛitti and 0 kha to
Pōturāju (village god) and one vṛitti to Sōmayadēva alongwith the taxes namely
āyam, pamgam, kānika and Darisana for the merit of his parents by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara,
mīsaragamḍa Kaṭāri Sāḷuva Kampayyadēva mahārāja, son of Avubhalayadēva mahārāja
and Ōbāyama.

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. B 255 of 1936.)
ANANTAPALLE, TADEPALLIGUDEM TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Pillar set up at the entrance into the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Vēṇugōpalasvāmi
temple.

It is dated Śaka 1383, Prajāpati, Kārttika śu. 12, Friday. The cyclic year is Vṛisha
not Pnajāpati and the day of the record corresponding to October 17, 1461 A.D.

It registers the gift of land measuring one puṭṭi along with the palm and mango
trees situated to the south of Ṟompicheṟuvu to the god Śrī Kēśavaraya Perumāḷu
at Arūṁḍi palli by Achinēni, the son of Chilkūri Kāchinēḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. B 207 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the northern wall of the kitchen in the Agastyēśvara svāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1464, Subhakṛit, Kārttīka śu. 11, corresponding to October 22,
Tuesday, 1482 A.D.

Registers the gift of 50 māḍas for maintaining the tiruvaḷike to the god
Agastīśvara for the merit of Aṅkaya by his brother Pōlireḍḍi and Basvireḍḍi, the
sons of Tallapareḍi Chinnapareḍi of Kaṁnāṭi. The gift is entrusted to the sthānikas
of the temple with the stipulation to maintain both morning tiruvaḷike and evening
tirukāpulu by purchasing the land from income derived from the source.

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. B 301 of 1936.)
BUKKAPAṬNAM, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone found in the land survey No. 414 to the east of the village.
It is dated Śaka 1405, • • • śu. 15, Lunar eclipse.

Badly damaged and mutilated. It records the date portion and the people of
Bukkapaṭnam.

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. B 318 of 1937.)
PEDDAPALLE, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a Garuḍa pillar set up in front of the Chennakēśvasvāmi temple.

This inscription is dated Śaka 1410, Saumya, Chaitra, ba. 30. The cyclic year,
month and day falls on 1489 A.D. and the date of the record corresponds to
1489 A.D.

Damaged and incomplete. Records that certain Yallāpregaḍa Devarāju presented
an (image of) Garutmanta to the temple of Kēśavanātha.

l.te: • • [saṁ • vatsa]ra caitra ba 30 ma nāṁḍu perupalli sīma ka • • •

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. B 311 of 1937.)
MUTTUPALLE AGRAHARAM, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa inside the Sitaramasvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 142[9], Raktākshi, Pushya • • • • • Uttarāyaṇa corresponding to
December 7, Saturday 1504 A.D.
Only date portion is legible.

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. B 217 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMLAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Vēṇugōpālaśvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1442, Vikrama, Jyēshtha śu. 7, Wednesday corresponding to
1520 A.D., May 27.

Registers the gift of land measuring kha 110 for the maintanance of a choultry
(feeding house) situated on the way leading to Tirumala for the maintanance to the
ascetics, Chātāni Vaishnavas, Brahmins, devotees (dāsānudāsalu), merchants of native
and foreign lands (sūradēsisu, paradēsulu) pilgrims of the Tirumala for the (festivals ?)
of Tiruvēngaḷanātha at Tirumala and also for dharmasatram at Chadupurāla by
Chanayya, son of Gurujepalli Narasappayya for the merit of Timmarusayya and
Gōvindayya.

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. B 320 of 1936.)
PĀLAGIRI, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone near the dhvaja-staṁbha in the Chennakēśava śvāmi temple.

This inscription is dated in the Śaka 1442, Vikrama, • • • • • bu. 3, Thursday,
Makarasaṅkramaṇa corresponding to 27th December, 1520 A.D.

Badly damaged, mentions the son (name not clear) of a certain Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Rāchirāja Basavayyadēva and registers some gifts made by him.

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. B 201 of 1938.)
ANIMELA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying on the south side of the Saṅgamēśvaraśvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1453, Khara, Vaiśākha, ba. 3[0], Monday, corresponding to
May 16 (f.d.t. °66) 1531 A.D.

Damaged. It registers the grant of toll-income on articles namely pōṁkalu (betel
nuts), miryālu (pepper) bellamu (jaggery), nuvulu (rice), cereals per one bag (gōnesaṁchi)
passing through the Chimia kanuma, Pulivendala kanuma and Gaṇḍi Kanuma by the
Pekkaṁḍru, the followers of Gaurēśvara, Gaṇēśvaradēva, to the temple of Saṅgamēśvara
and Vīrabhadra at Animala.

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. B 202 of 1938.)
ANIMELA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying on the South side of the Saṅgamēśvara temple.

It is dated Śaka 1453, Khara, Vaiśākha, ba. 10, Monday, corresponding to May
15th, 1531 A.D. f.d.t. °66.

Registers the gift of toll income (magama) on several articles of merchandise
passing through the trade routes such as the Gaṇḍikanuma, Chintaknuma etc., by the
merchant guilds, for the maintanance of lamps and offerings to the gods Sangamēśvara
and Vīrabhadra at Animela. In the euology of the guild, Jainagiri is mentioned as the
resident of Bhikshāvṛitti Ayya.

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. B 304 of 1937.)
BHAṬṬIPRŌLU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the proper right pillar of the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Viṭṭhalēśvara temple.

It is dated in the Śaka 1460, Hēmaḷaṁbi, Phālguṇa, ba. 10 corresponding to
Sunday, February, 24, 1538 A.D. (current).

The first face of the record states that Vuppalapāṭi Timmanāyuḍu renewed the
gift of a land of 1 kha measured with the specific measuring rod called kēsaripāṭi
which was originally granted by Vishṇuvaradhana chakravarti, for maintaining the
perpetual lamp in the temple of Viṭṭhalēśvara at Bhaṭṭiprōlu.

The second face refers to the re-construction of maṇḍapa to the temple of Śrī
Viṭṭhalēśvara mahādēva in the cyclic year chitrabhānu. The third face, written in a
Āṭavēladi meter, refers, to the worship to the God.

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. B 275 of 1936.)
INJĒḌU, KOILKUNTLA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up under a margosa tree planted to the north of the Veṅkaṭēśvara temple.

This inscription is dated in Śaka 1461, Vikāri, Jyēshṭha ba. 30, corresponding to
1539 A.D. June 16, Monday.

It records the construction of a step well (naḍabhāvi) to the temple of
Vēṅkaṭādri at Injēḍu by sthala-karaṇaṁ Paṇayya, son of Kasvarāju and registers the
gift of wet land measuring 1/2 kha in extent situated at Liṁgaṁdinne for maitaining
the well and 1/4 kha of wet land for maitaining the offerings and worship to the god
Vēṅkaṭādri by certain Pāpināyiḍu, son of Māchināyiḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. B 301 of 1938.)
VELDURTI, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab lying in Survey No. 13, situated about two kilometers to the
west of the village.

It is dated in Śaka 1471, Saumya, Śrāvaṇa., ba. 30 corresponding to 1549 A.D.,
August 22, Tuesday.

It registers the renewal of the gifted paddy land mesuring one yindumu and one
tūmu situated near the Amkkireḍḍipalle and garden in front of the house. for
maintaining vōjarikas to Vēbulōju, the son of Vaḍla Bālōju by mahānāyamkācharyya
Kūnapula Immaḍi Basivināyaka. The gift is said to have been earlier given to the
village carpenter-ship (vāḍla-ūḍla-ūḷḷa-vōjarikamu) by the donor.

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. B 205 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the Kitchen in the Agastyēśvara temple.

This epigraph is dated in Śaka 147[2], Sādhāraṇa, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 11 corresponding to
July 24, 1550 A.D.

It registers the gift derived from the Vīramushṭhi by Siddha Bhikshavitti
Nandēla Virayya for lamp offering to the temple of Agastyēśvara. The donee
Nandēla Virayya is endowed with the titles like Khētāmbara guṇḍuganḍa Bijanarāya
māmardana shaḍdarsana stāpanāchāryya.

l.te: ·agunēṭi sādhāraṇa saṁvasta(tsa)ra śrāvaṇa śu 11 lu śrīpa¨rvva
dēla vīrayyaṁgāru yiccina kānā(ṇā)ci kramameṭlaṁnnanu mī-
varu tappinānu divāsṛtivi mahattuku taputa bhikṣā-

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. B 316 of 1938.)
GŌRANṬLA, KARNOOL TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Base of the dhvajastaṁbha set up in front of the Vīrabhadrasvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1474, Parīdhāvi, Chaitra, śu. 3 corresponding to Monday,
March, 28, 1552 A.D.

It registers the gift of the Vīramushṭhi-pannu, which is supposed to be collected
from the village Gōranṭla situated in the Dōni-sīma. by three Vīramūshṭhis namely
Viramushthi Hucha Vīraya, Avarada Kamcha Vīraya and Śivayōgi Liṅgaya with the
permission of Śiva Sidhabhikshāvritti ayyavāru. The entire gift is given to the god
Vīrabhadra.

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. B 209 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the inside of the west prākāra wall in the Agastyēśvarasvāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1475, Pramādīcha, Āśvija, śu. 6, corresponding to September
13, Wednesday, 1553 A.D.

Registers an agreement arrived at between Pedamalōju, Chinamallōju, sons of
Agasāla Komḍōju, Basvaya, the son of Papana and Pāpōju son of Chadipirēla papoju,
Siddhayya, son of Yaṟalingōju to share their rights and incomes (kāṇichi and ōjarika)
at the ratio of three-fourth (3/4) and one-fourth (1/4) respectively derived from
elevan villages viz., Kamalāpuram, Chaḍi(piṭ)rālu, Tuṁmaluru etc.

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. B 279 of 1938.)
SAMBAṬŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the kalyāṇamaṇḍapa in the south-west corner of the Chennakēśava temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1476, Ānanda, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 10 corresponding to August 8,
Wednesday, 1554 A.D.

It states that the Kalyāṇamaṇḍapa was built by Bhōjanapalle Śrīnivāsayyangāru
for the purpose of conducting the festivals of the god Kēśava perumal at Sambatūru
alias Śrī Bhāshyapuram.

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. B 293 of 1936.)
MADDIKEṞA, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab lying in the roof of the ruined temple of Vīrabhadra in front of the house
of Jamēdār Timmappa Nāyuḍu.

It is dated in Śaka 1484, Duṁdubhi, Māgha, ba. 14 corresponding to 1563 A.D.
Feruary, 21, Sunday.

Damaged. Registers the gift of two vaillage Maddikera and Timmapuram, an
agrahāra attached to Maddikera by Ajātivīraśaiva Sidhabhikshāvṛitti-Ayyavāru for
worship and offering during the festival of Kārttīka to the god Vīrabhadra.

l.te: ṁdubhi saṁvatsara māṣu ba 14 lu śrīsvayaṁbhū śrīliṁgga cakra-
hāliṁgadēvuni saṁnidhini siṁhāsanārūḍhulai vuṁḍi maddikera śrīvī-

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. B 223 of 1936.)
KŪCHENAPALLI, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the ruined temple of Śiva.

It is dated in Śaka 1498, Dhātu, Vaiśākha, su. 10 corresponding to 1576 A.D. April
8, Sunday.

It records the consecration of the god Gōpala-kṛishṇamma at Kūchanapalli and
the gift of land 3 kuchaḍlu (kunchālu ?) by Dīpānāyumḍu of Poṭapalli for maintaining
the amṛitapaḍi service to the god. The second side contains three imprecatory verse
‘svadattam’.

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. B 212 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Vēṇugōpālasvāmi temple.

This incription is dated Śaka 1501(3), Vṛishu, Āshāḍha, śu. 11, Irregular. Since the
cyclic year Vṛisha falls on Śaka 1503, as per the Indian Ephemeris, the date is
corresponding to June, 12, 1581 A.D.

Registers the gift of land as mānya in the village Chadupurēla by Bomalāṭa
Amṛita kavi Peddayya Chandārayya to Chandupurēla Pedda Chiṭṭayya for maintaining
the puppet show as per gaṇa and yati principles in the village and also for his
livilihood.

cadupurēla pedda ciṭṭayyaku yiccina tha¨rmaśāsanaṁ [|*] nīvu tera nāṭakaṁ gaṇayati

cadupurēla grāmāna mākālava tasaku ·āṁni ·anagalaraṁgāmuna nīvu paraṁparaṁgānu
cadupurēlagrāma • • ·istimi [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. B 321 of 1937.)
PEDDAPULIVAṞṞU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pillar inside the Varadarājaśvāmi temple.
It is dated Śaka 1512, Vijaya, Chaitra, śu. 10, Thursday. The cyclic year is Vīkriti.

Records that certain [bi]ṭlum Goravi Gaṁginēmḍu reconsecrated the god Varadarāja
(in the temple).

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. B 323 of 1937.)
PEDAPULIVAṞṞU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the temple of Narēndrēśvarasvāmi.
The record mentions the Śaka 1512, Vijaya, Chaitra, śu. 10, Thursday, Irregular.
States the linga of Narēndrēśvara was reconsecrated by Bitḷuṁ Goravi Ganginēṁḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. B 208 of 1936.)
DŌMALAGUṆḌA (NEAR GAṆḌIGANAMALA), VINUKONDA TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the forest about 1.5 k.m., to the north-east of the village.
It is dated in Śaka 1513, (wrong), Śubhakṛit, Āshādha, śu. 11.

It registers the gift of land with tax-free facilities to Pedamalla bhaṭṭlu of
Sattenapalle, the son of Kaṁchhābattu and the grand son of Mallabattu of Māruṭla
gōtra by Rāmana Tiṁmāreḍḍi, the son of Basvi reḍḍi and the grandson of
Mallāreḍḍi of Valluṭla gōtra.

inscription.head:
No. 213.
(A.R. No. B 206 of 1936.)
DŌMALAGUNDA (NEAR GAṆḌIGANAMALA), VINUKONDA TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A slab set up near the well to the east of the village.

This is dated in Śaka 1516(7), Manmatha, (Nija Jyēshṭha), ba. 7, Thursday
corresponding to 19th June, 1595 A.D.

It records the gift of a land called Kuchhala Chēnu lying in the field of
Dōmalaguṇḍa of Vinikoṇḍa-sīma to Nāgābhattu, son of Sattinapalli Kammā battuḍu
for digging the well in the fields of Bokkamuntalapāḍu by the donor Nīlapa, the son
of Lingamanāyaka and the grandson of Gummaḷḷapu Nīlapanāyaka of Māruṭla
gōtra. The boundaries of the gift land are mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. B 274 of 1936.)
INJĒḌU, KOILKUNTLA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
A slab built into the wall of a room, inside the prākāra of the temple of
Venkaṭēśvara.

This is dated in Śaka 1522, Vikāri, Śrāvaṇa, ba. 13, Wednesday corresponding to
1600 A.D. August 26, f.d.t. 42.

Registers a grant of land made to the temple of Tiruvengaḷanātha of Injēḍu by
a certain Akki Reḍḍi, situated at the entrance (mogatala) of the village of Pinayama.
He is stated to have consecrated the imagess of the goddess Śridēvi and the Āḷvārs
therein.

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. B 265 of 1938.)
UPPALŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Rāmalingēśvara temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1534, Parīdhāvi, Chaitra, śu. 2. corresponding to 1612 A.D.
March 24, Tuesday.

Badly damaged and worn out. It seems to record the gift of daśavanda land for
the construction and maintanance of a tank.

l.te: ṭi parithāvi saṁvatsara caita śu 2 lu

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. B 236 of 1938.)
KAMALĀPURAM, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Pātāḷa Sōmēśvarasvāmi temple.

This inscription is dated in the Śaka year 1539, Pingaḷa Śrāvaṇa ba. 5, corresponding
to August 11, Monday, 1617 A.D.

Records the gift of four tūmus of wet land which is cultivable in two seasons
(irugāru) at Kottakāluva, Reḍḍikāluva, Tāḷḷakāluva and Prātakāluva by Aḷiya Avubhalrāju,
son of mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Jillēḷḷa Rangapatirājayya, at the request of Gāṁgareḍḍi,
son Nagireḍḍi of Kāmulāpuram, for maintaining the worships and offerings to the
goddess Pārvvatīdēvi at Kāmulāpuram.

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. B 285 of 1936.)
KUṄKANŪRU, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar set up in front of the Āñjanēya temple.

This is dated in the cyclic year Sidhārthi, Kārttīka. śu. 15. The date of the record
is corresponding to Śaka year 1541 = 1619 A.D. November 11, Thursday.

Registers an agreement (kavulu) made between the Kāpulu and mahā maṇḍalēśvara
Komāra Rāmarājayya, the son of Pōṭipāṭi Komāra Timmarājayadēva mahārāja, to
plough and spouting the seed (bijāvani) in the field called rēgaḍubīḍu situated at
Kuṁkanūru, with an agreement to pay the fees at the rate (?) of Vaga 3 in the
third year total cost of 7 maḍas for whole three years to the Kāmpus.

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. B 169 of 1938.)
TIṆṆAṆŪR, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHENGLEPUT DISTRICT, TAMIL NADU.
inscription.p:
East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Bhaktavatsala Perumal temple.
This dated in Śaka 1554, Āngīrasa, Vaiśākha., śu • • • • .

Incomplete and registers the gift of land (details are not mentioned) for worships
and offerings, viz., Dadyōdanaṁ, Tiruvisam, Dōśapaḍi, Appupaḍi, Tirumāle vaḍapaḍi,
to the god Bhaktavatsala perumāl situated at Tiruniṇṟavū by China Ōbalarāja,
son of Ōbarāja and grand son of Obuḷarāja, Kāśyapa-gōtra and Yajuśakha and
Āpastaṁbḥa sūtra.

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. B 227 of 1936.)
MALLEVĀGU, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab, Survey No. 675/3 in Lālipuram (hamlet) of the village Mallevāgu.

It is dated in Śaka (wrong for Kali) 4883, Bhāva, Jyēshṭha śu. 15, Sunday. This
may be assigned to the 17th century on the palaeography. The details of the date
regularly correspond to 1634 A.D., June 1.

It registers the gift of wet land of 1 kuchchala in extent situated in Malagumtala
to Sāvāju Rāmābattuḍu by Verlamti Peddaya.

l.te: • ṁgga kuccalacēnu nīvu bōkkamuṁtala pāṭi pōlaṁlōsu nu·i tavvi-

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. B 238 of 1936.)
RĒMḌICHERLA, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the well situated behind the Śiva temple.

This is dated in Śaka 1557, Bhāva Kārttika, ba. 15 corresponding to 1634 A.D.
(expired), November 10, Monday.

It states that certain Rāmānāyuḍu got constructed the wells with the help of the
people and restricted to pay the tax on these wells, to the officials, who used to
collect the taxes on these wells enormously for drawing the water at Remiḍicherla.
Remiḍicherla is situated in Vinikonḍasīma.

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. B 212 of 1936.)
GUMMANAṀPŪḌI, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the well to the south of the choultry.

It is dated in Śaka 1564, Chitrabhānu, Kārttīka, śu. 12 corresponding to October
24, Monday, 1642 A.D.

It records the digging of a step well (naḍa-bāvi) in the center of the village
Guṁmalaṁpāḍu by the son (name lost) of Sarvvanēḍu, and the grandson of
Vaṁkāyalapāṭi Timī[raḍi] of Vēlupūrigōtra.

l.te: citrabhānu saṁvvatsara kā¨rttīka śu 12 lu vaṁkkāyā-

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. B 5 of 1937.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT, TAMIL NADU.
inscription.p:
On a slab fixed in the Yajñaśālai-maṇḍapa on the north side of the second
prākāra in the temple of Ranganāthasvāmi.

It is dated in Śaka 1572, (expressed in Kaṭapaya system vasumtyēḥ) Nandana
Vaiśākha, śu. bhadrā (8), corresponding to April 5, Monday, 1652 A.D.

Refers to the construction of a big and elevated Yajñaśālai in the temple of Śrī
Raṅg(rāja) by Rāmānuja mahāmuni.

edition.p: (Published in South Indian Inscription Volume XXIV Inscriptions. No. 534, p. 515.)

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. B 273 of 1938.)
SAMBAṬŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On two slabs set up to front of the Chennakēśava temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1575, (wrong) Plavaṅga Āśvīja, śu. 10 Tuesday, (Angāraka-
vāra) Śrāvaṇa Abhijit muhūrta. The Śaka and cyclic year do not tally with each
other. Pālaeographically this can be assigned to the 17th century A.D. Hence, on the
basis of internal evidence of the date i.e., nakshatra, muhūrta and the week day the
Śaka year can be fixed to 1533 and the date corresponds to September 24 f.d.t. °45
1661 A.D.

It states that the god commanded two persons namely Naṁgappa and Gaṅgayamma
of Duppalli, having hitherto been propitiated under the name Chennakēśava. He (the
god) wished to be worshipped thereafter under the name Lakshmi-Nārāyaṇasvāmi
and that accordingly they (Niṁgappa and Gaṅgayamma) came to Sambaṭūru from
Ghaṇḍikōṭa, and renamed the god in the presence of āchārya-purushas and Brāhmaṇas.
It is also stated that the money collected from the devotees and the pilgrims should
go half to the administration (nagari-vāru) of the village, and the other half to the
temple, with which the ‘Rāmānuja-kūṭa’ and other charities should be maintained.

l.te: gaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrīṁ jēyunū ||

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. B 267 of 1938.)
PEDDACHAPALLE, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa of the Agastyēśvara svāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1584, Śubhakṛit, Mārgaśira, ba. 13, corresponding to November
28, 1662 A.D.

Registers the lease given by Havāldāru Hamīru Saiṁdu Hussēnu-Sāhebu to the
Kāṁpus and Karaṇams of the village of Chappelli. It records that the land area of
Chappalli village and other villages (Saṁgaḍi grāmās) was measured earlier by a
pole (ghaḍa) of non-standard length resulting the reduction of their original extent
and the consequent desertion of the village by its tenets. The Sāhebu ordered this
official Ānanda Dharmmāji Aṁtōpantulu to re-measure the said village with a
standard pole, measuring 24, cubits (mūras) and also the reḍḍis and karaṇams were
asked to follow the same standard measurement for measuring the lands.

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. B 222 of 1936.)
KOTTALŪRU, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar lying in survey No. 234 about 1/2 k.m. to the east of the village.

It is dated in the cyclic year Viśvāvasu, Jyēshṭha ba. 7, Thursday. The record may
be assigned to the 17th century on the basis of palaeography. During this century the
cyclic year Viśvāvasu corresponds to 1604 and 1665 A.D. However, the details of the
date of the epiraph regularly correspond to 1665 A.D., May 25, f.d.t. 24.

It records the planting the pillars by Pedanēṇḍu, son of Tomṭakūra Mallinēdu
Poṭḷūru.

l.te: śubhamastu [||*] viśvāvasu jēṣṭha ba[7]gu poṭlūri tōṁṭakūramallinēni

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. B 252 of 1938.)
MĪRĀPURAM, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragmentary slab lying in the field about a furlong to the north of the village.

The record is dated in the cyclic year Viśvāvasu, Māgha, śu. 13, Wednesday, and
can be assigned to 17th century on the basis of palaeography. Hence the date of the
record may be corresponded to 1666 A.D. Feruary, 7.

Registers the lease of land in the village Mīrāpuram. Saṁbaṭūru, Pāsīkalupādu
given by Havaldar Hussain Sāheb.

l.te: kū • ra parastalāla • • • prakāraṁ kuṁdara bī

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. B 327 of 1938.)
PRĀṬAKŌTA, NANDIKOTKURU TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Chennakēśava temple.

It is dated in the Śaka year 1590 (wrong for 1589), Plavanga, Mārgasīra ba. 7,
corresponding to November 27, Wednesday, 1667 A.D. (current).

It refers to a land deed (dāna-patram) which states that the gift of lands
measuring three khas of Taṭimānirēgaḍi land made by the reḍḍlu, karṇams to the
god Cheṁnakēśvarasvāmi of Prāṁtakōṭa.

l.te: ṭī māni rēgaḍi kha 3 ê 1 • • • nēlakhaṁ • •

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. B 291 of 1936.)
MADDIKERA, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
A pillar set up in a row at the top of the flight of steps in front of the
Chennakēśava temple.

This is dated in the Śaka 1603, Durmati, Śrāvaṇa ba. 12, Monday corresponding
to 1681 A.D. August 18.

It records the gift of wet-land of sowing capacity of one tūmu and four muntas
to four persons Jaṁggam, Veṁggayya Śiddaya and Pāpaya, the sons of a stone
mason (Kāsē) Chiṭṭi pottu Akkā battu with the permission of the village officials
Reḍḍi and Karaṇams for planting the Vuṭṭlakaṁbālu in Chenakēśava temple.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. B 305 of 1936.)
POṬṬIPĀḌU, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab lying near the dhvajastaṁbha in front of the Śiva temple in the
compound of the Narasimha temple.

This record is dated in the cyclic year Tāraṇa, Mārgaśira, śu. 12. Palaeographically
the inscription can be assigned to 18th century. Hence the date may corresponds to
(1) November 27, Monday, 1705 A.D., (2) December 5, 1764 A.D.

Registers the grant of lands to Pedayaddulaya by the Reḍḍi and Karaṇams of
Poṭṭipāḍu, with the permission of Bāla Veṅkatreḍḍi, in view of the forcible
occupation of his lands by the Taṭimarri people. This is drafted by the Karaṇam
Rāmadāsu with the permission of Nāgayya and Chiṁnāgaya.

edition.h4: TEXT. The sun and a crescent are carved at the top of the

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. B 262 of 1938.)
PEDDANAPĀḌU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Madanagōpāla svāmi temple.

This is dated Śaka 1635, Vijaya, Vaiśākha śu. 15 corresponding to April 29,
Wednesday, 1713 A.D.

It records the gift of silver and gold idols (rajata and Kāṁchana vigrahamulu)
of Madanagōpāla swami made by Timma-reḍḍi of Peddanapāḍu. He also set up the
dvajastaṁbha as well as dīpastaṁbha in the temple complex and performed the
garuḍōtsava for the deity.

l.te: nāma saṁvvatsara vaiśākha śu 15 lu peddana pā-

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. B 297 of 1936.)
ANANTĀPURAM, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab used as a mortar near the temple of Narasiṁhasvāmi about two miles to
the south-west of the village.

It is dated in the Śaka 1635 (wrong for 1639), Hēmaḷambi, Vaiśākha śu. 2,
Aṁggāraka vāra (Tuesday) corresponding to 1717 A.D. April 2.

It states that the stone mortar (rōlu) was caused to be made by Nārayana, the
son of [ā*]chāri Karamala Chinamārasimbu, who is the son of Venkataya and
Venkaṭayya described as the son of Nārāyaṇa, the Karaṇam of Anantāpuram.

l.te: ruḍu • • ya vīrula niṁmatini sōhastaṁ veṁkaṭavrālu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. B 313 of 1936.)
INDUKŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the village Chāvaḍi.

This record is dated in Śaka 1647 (wrong for Śaka 1649), Plava, Māgha śu. 15,
Monday corresponding to 1727 A.D. January 23.

Records the construction of a new temple for the god Chaṁnakēśava, consecration
of the principal deity and Utsava vigrahās and also made the gift of lands situated
at Kīrttipallee for maintaining the lamp and offering to the god by Rājaśrī
Gōvadhanadāsu of Chandragiri. It further records the appointment of Mirāśidār
Bollavaraṁ Kishṭaya Narasaya, as archaka for the temple, with a remuneration of
one ga per month. It further records the gift of land situated at Naḍimidbōva to the
main architect (Doḍḍavōju) Vaḍla Guruvābaṭṭu of Yiṁdupūri.
