inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. B 279 of 1936.)
DĀMAGAṬLA, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab fixed in the ceiling of the temple of Chennakēśavasvāmi.
It is dated in Śaka 1650, Plāvaṅga, Chaitra śu. 10, March 10, 1727 A.D.

It records the offerings of ten thousand namaskārams daily to the God Cheṁnna
Kēśavarasvāmi at Dāmgaṭla by certain Śarabhanna, the stone cutter (Kāśutal) of
Yallāla.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. B 284 of 1936.)
VIPANAGANḌLA, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab on the parapet wall near the Śiva temple, about a furlong to the west of
the village.

It is dated in Śaka 1659, Naḷa (wrong for Pingaḷa), Vaiśākha śu. 10 corresponding
to April 28, Thursday, 1737 A.D.

It refers to the construction of a well in the a garden by Vīramarāju, the great
grandson of Pāvirāju and grandson of Doḍḍamarāju of Kāśyapagōtra and
Katyāyanasūtrā.

l.te: naḷanāma saṁvatsara vaiśākha śu 10 lu śrī-

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. B 290 of 1936.)
MADDIKEṞA, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the village Chāvaḍi.

It is dated Śaka in 1661, Siddhārti, Māgha, śu. 2, corresponding to 1740 A.D.,
January 19, Saturday.

It records an assurance (Bharavasa) to the mercantile communities viz., merchants
belonging to Maddimeṟa and Chappanadēśālavāru by Śrī Rājarāmappanāyaka
Rājarāmappa nāyaka is described as the son of Bujjappanāyaka and the grandson of
Dēvarakoṇḍa Rāmappa nāyaka. The donor Rāmappanāyaka belonged to the lunar
race and Mārkanḍēya gōtra and bears the title Dēśingarāya-rājya-sthāpanāchārya
(the establisher of the kingdom of Dēśingarāya).

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. B 358 of 1937.)
MUTTUKŪRU, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the house of the village Munsiff, Virī-gowḍa.
It is dated Śaka 1663, Durmmati, corresponding to 1742 A.D.

It records the setting up of the porch at the entrance (vākiṭa-mōgasāla) by
Cherakūri Timmagauvunivāru.

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. B 251 of 1938.)
MĪRĀPURAM, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On one of the beams in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Rāmaliṅgēśvara temple.

It is dated in Śaka 166(9), Akshaya, Phālguṇa, ba. 15, corresponding to February
28, Saturday 1747 A.D.

It is a dharma śāsanam and registers the gift of lands and gardens to Śrī Mallāla
sēku for maintaining the daily, fortnight and monthly festivals and also daily
offerings to the god Rāmalingasvāmi at Mīrāpuram by the adhikāris, Reḍḍikaraṇans
of Mīrāpuram. The signatories of the record are Śrīpati rājavaradaya, Rājagōpālu,
Krishṇya and same gift (?) is said to have been to the sculptors Veṁkatarāmuḍu and
Vumāmahēśvaralu.

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. B 290 of 1938.)
VALASAPALLE, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone built into the wall of the ‘burz’.

It is dated in the Śaka 1689, Sarvvajit, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15, corresponding to 1767 A.D.
August 9, Sunday.
Records the construction of bastion (buruzu).

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. B 250 of 1938.)
MĪRĀPURAM, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a beam in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Rāmaliṅgēśvara svāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1666, Akshya (wrong for Raktāksha) Jyēshṭha, ba. 7,
corresponding to May 22, Monday, 1744 A.D.

Slightly damaged. Registers the gift of land measuring 2 yidumus situated on the
eastern side of the village Mīrāpuram by Rajāśri Lālānadha·amalu, (Lāla Amantul)
the amina of Sammuturu in view of the loans taken by Yarramaredi, son of
Dūsireḍi Timmareḍi for the construction of Īśvara temple.

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. B 357 of 1937.)
MUTTUKŪRU, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set in front of the Village Munisiff Vīrēgauḍas house.

It is dated in Śaka 1710, Kīlaka, Māgha, ba. 10, corresponding to Thursday 19,
February, 1789 A.D.
Refers to the setting up (of a pillar ?) by a certain Ayapa gavuḍa (Gauda).

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. B 315 of 1937.)
ŌLĒRU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a tablet fixed on a dilapitated tomb near the church in the village.
It is dated in Christian year 1790 A.D., Sādharaṇa, Āśvija, śu. 12, Thursday.

It records the death of Inayya (a Christian convert), son of Niḍamāmiḷḷa Rājappa
and Akkamma, an employee (prānsu pādushā) under the French Government at
Pudicheri-paṭṭana i.e., present Puducheri of Tamil Nadu. A French version of the
record is engraved below the above Telugu varsion.

l.te: tāraṇasaṁ | nijajyēṣṭha ba | 2 | gu | belēru lō

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. B 259 of 1938.)
PĀYASAMPALLE, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone pillar in front of the Chennakēśava temple.

It is dated in Śaka 1720, Kālayukti, Nija-śrāvaṇa, śu. 15, corresponding to 1798,
A.D. August 26, Sunday.

Records the set up of lamp stad (dīpastaṁbam) by Pedagurvireḍi, the son of
Vīrareḍi and the grand son of Nala Gurvi reḍḍi at Pāyasaṁpalle. He is said to the
devotee of Vīrabhadra swāmi.

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. B 295 of 1936.)
MADDIKEṞA, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the dhvaja-staṁbha in front of the temple of Māddamma.

This record is dated in the Śaka 1731, Śukla, Āśvija, śu. 10, corresponding to 1809
A.D. October 18, Wednesday.

Records that Mallikārjuna Nāyanivāru, son of Krishṇarāya and grandson of
Dēvanakoṇḍa Ramappanāyanivāru made a new image of the goddess Mādhavi and
got installed it in a temple at Maddikera as the original one is damaged.

edition.p: *) This inscription is engraved in florah design.

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. B 214 of 1936.)
INUMEḶḶA, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in front of the Bhāvanārāṇasvāmy temple situated to the
west of the village.

This inscription is dated in the Śaka 1730, Vibhava, Māgha, śu. Pūrnima,
corresponding to January 31, Tuesday, 1809 A.D.

States that Veṁkkaṭaramaṇaya (Veṁkkaṭaramaṇamma), the frist wife of Rāja
Veṁkaṭaguṁḍarāyaniṁgāru got polished the golden kalaśa and also conscrated the
chakra of the pinnacle. It also records the establishment of a garden and construction
of a tank in the garden situated on the eastern side of the village in the name of
Bangārayya (Bangāramma), the younger daughter of Ravaṇamma, the first wife of
Venkataguṇḍārāyaḍu It further records the specific qualities of rice, oil etc., supplied
for the daily worship of the god thrice a day and also for the maintenance of the
nine day festivals (navāmika-vutsavanu) to god obviously Bhavānārāyaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. B 230 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the front wall of the Manchālamma temple, about a kilometer to the west
of the village.

It is dated in Śaka 1737, Yuva, Vaiśākha, śu. 5, corresponding to May 15,
Saturday, 1815 A.D.

Records that the garbhagṛiha constructed to the temples of Pālēṭaṁṁa and
Manchālamma by Gangi reḍḍi and Linga reḍḍi, the sons of Chiṁnagaṇḍi(gi) reḍi
and grandsons of Gōllapalle Badde reḍḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. B 230 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the front wall of the Manchālamma temple, about a kilometer to the west
of the village.
It is dated in Śaka 1737, Yuva, Vaiśākha, śu. 5, corresponding to 1815 A.D.

Damaged and incomplete. Seems to record the gift to the goddesses Pālēṭaṁṁa
and Mañchālamma by the pūjāri Nālagaya.

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. B 258 of 1938.)
PANDIḶḶAPALLE, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the well on the road side to the south of the village.

It is dated in Śaka 1750, Sarvadhāri, Vaiśākha, śu. 15, corresponding to 1828 A.D.
April 29, Tuesday.

Records the digging of stone well obviously to provide the irrigation facilities to
the 110 acres of land having revenue number (?) 50 situated in between the eastern
side of the Venkaṭāchalapati temple and the southern side of the village Pamdiḷḷapalle
of Kāyalāpuram taluk. Koṁḍḍāreḍḍi, the son of China Mallareḍḍi, was the polemere
mirāsidār of Bhūmayyapalli.

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. B 288 of 1938.)
UPPALŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a stone in front of the Vīrabhadrasvāmi temple situated in survey No. 543,
to the west of the village.

It is dated in Śaka 1753, Khara, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 5, Mamdavāra (Saturday) corresponding
to 1831 A.D., August 12, Friday f.d.t. °80.

Refers to the sevice (sēva) by a woman Pullaṁmma, wife of Pullabattu,
Pullabattu is described as the son of Vīrabattu and the grandson Vīrabattu of
Vēlpucharla.

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. B 288 of 1938.)
KANAMALACHEṞUVU, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the laṁbaḍi colony on the way to Palukūru.

It is dated in Śaka 1770, Saumya, Vaiśākha, śu. 6, corresponding to April 28,
Saturday, 1848 A.D.

It records the settlement of the boundary dispute between the village Palukūru
and Kanamala-cheṟuvu by Mēstaru Chenasu-dora and Hassamkhān-sāheb, the Tahsildāru
of Vinikonḍa Talukā and setting up of the boundary stone on the west bank of the
Sōmulavāgu with consent of the involved parties.

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. B 287 of 1938.)
UPPALŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab set up in front of the Vīrabhadrasvāmi temple situated to the
west of the village.

It is dated in Śaka 1772, Saumya, Āshāḍha, ba. 10, corresponding to 1849 A.D.,
June 30, Saturday.

States that Pullabattuḍu son of Charlabaḍla Vīranna and his wife Pullamma
constructed the sanctuṁ sanctorun (garbhagaḍi) and Vitappa son of Chinvīranna
engraved the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. B 308 of 1937.)
GŪḌAVALLI, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the wall is front of the Lakshmīnarasiṁha svāmi temple.

It is dated in Śaka 4959 (wrong for kali 4959), Kāḷayukti, Vaiśākha, śu. 13,
corresponding to Sunday, April 25, 1858 A.D.

Records the reconstruction of damaged temple to Lakshmīnarsiṁha by Muttayyadāsu,
the son of Goḍavartti Narasayya, the resident of Kanagāla village.

l.te: gā jarugutū vuṁḍḍē gūḍamallalō śrīlakṣmīnarasiṁhmasvāmi vāri
kōvela jī¨rnamte vunnaṁduna kanagālakāpurastuḍu goḍava¨rtti nara-

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. B 317 of 1937.)
ŌLĒRU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in front of the elementary school.

It is dated in Śaka 1785, Kali 4264, Duṁdubhi, Vaiśākha, śu. 15, Tuesday, svāti,
corresponding to 1863 A.D., June 22.

States that a merchant Koṇḍappaseṭṭi, the resident of Chennapaṭṭna (present
Chennai) purchased a plot of land along with a well in the Śaka year Raudri,
Adhika Jyēshṭha, ba. 10, Thursday equivalent to 1850 A.D. October and got constructed
a Choultry in the same place. It is handed over to one Kākumānu Vīraṁnnabaṭṭu,
the resident of Kolluru of Krishna District for the use of the Brahmins, Kshatriya
and Vaisya and the other pilgrims, who are coming from the pilgrims centers like
Kāśi and Rāmēśvaram. It further states that this gift should not be sold out by the
donor or his succesor or kinsmen. If any body violates the rule, the government
should order for the restoration of the said gift.

l.te: ṣṇā ḍisṭrikṭu tenāli tālukālō cērna kollūru

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. B 263 of 1936.)
PEDDACHINTAKUNTA, SIRUVEL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the mukha-maṇḍapa of the Sāmbaśi-vēśvara temple.
It is dated in the cyclic year Śaka, Ashāḍha, śu. 15, Thursday.

It refers to the land sale deed. It records the sharing of one-fourth (nālavapōlu)
of the produce of lands belonging to Anna-jiyya to Javemula Naga-Jiyya and further
states that the sons of Annajiyya namely Sidda-jiyya, Mara-jiyya and Kampa-jiyya
had agreed to continue remit as per stipulation of their father. The transaction was
written with the permission of Pochama-reḍḍi, Kasavi-reḍḍi and several others. The
record was engraved by Jogirāju and Aubalarāju and prepared by Bairajumalya.

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. B 288 of 1936.)
KUNKANŪRU, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Kēśvasvāmi temple.

This mutilated record can be assigned to 15th century A.D. on the basis of
paleography though it mentions the cyclic year. Pramādi and Chaitra, śu.
Badly damaged and seems to record an agrahāra called Śrīraṅgapura.

l.te: pramādi saṁvatsara caitra śu • • lu śrīmatu • •

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. B 72 of 1938.)
JAMBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the door jamb at the northern entrance to the fourth prākāra of
Jambukēśvara temple.
It is dated in the cyclic year Bhava, Mārgaḷi.

Engraved in modern characters. States that the gate (vākili) was erected by
Akhilāṇḍayya, son of Lingarāju, for the goddess Akhilāṇḍēśvari.

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. B 325 of 1937.)
PEDAPULIVARRU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Second face of a pillar in the temple of Narēndrēśvarasvāmi.

This inscription can be assigned to 15th-16th centuries on the basis of paleography
though it mentions the Cyclic year Dhāta, Māgha.

Damaged. Refers to the consecration of Vīrēśvara by Jaluḍu Gaṁggiseṭṭi for the
merit of his parents Linggisetti and Nāgammayamma.

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. B 225 of 1938.)
DONḌAPĀḌU (hamlet of Valasapalle), KAMALAPURAM TALUK,
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the Gōpālasvāmi temple.
This record is dated in the cyclic year Pramōdūta, Śrāvana, śu. 7, Friday.

It registers remission of tax at the rate of 10 (dasavanda) for the land called
mīnumānya situated in front of the tank in Doṇḍpāḍu by Vōbulayyadēva-Mahārāja,
son of Maha Maharāja, to Singamareḍḍi Timmaya and Kaṁchchena Timmaya, for
having built the tank. The village Doṇḍpāḍu is stated to be situated in Gaṇḍikōṭasīma
and to have been given to the donor by Sāḷuva Gōvindayya. The dasavanda is equal
to one ēndumu (measurement) of land.

l.te: pramōdūta saṁvvatsara śrāvaṇa śuddha 7 śu śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēśvara ma

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. B 169 A of 1938.)
TIṆṆAṆŪR, TIRUVALLUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT, TAMIL NADU
inscription.p:
On the East wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Bhaktavatsala perumal temple.
This damaged inscription mentions the Cyclic year Pramādūta, Chaitra, śu. 10.

Damaged and Incomplete. Mentions Ōbuḷarāja, son of Ōbḷarāja of Āpastambha
sūtra and Yajuśākha.

l.te:
śubhamastu [||*] svasti [||*] śrīvijayābhyudaya śālī(li)vāhana śakavaruṣaṁbulu veyi • • • • •
• ·agunēṁṭi pramaudūda saṁvatsara caitraśuddha 10 lu

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. B 56 of 1938.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TIRUCHINOPOLY TALUK, TIRUCHINOPOLY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Śankarālaya.
This can be dated to 17th century on the basis of palaeography.

States that the jayastaṁbha was installed by Sadaśivamakhin which enables the
god names of Lord Śiva i.e., Hara, Hari and Sūrya.

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. B 257 of 1936.)
ANANTAPALLE, TADEPALLIGUDEM TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the shrine of Vighnēśvara in front of the Vēgi Lingēśvara temple.
This is dated in the cyclic year Nandana, Śrāvana, śu. 10, Sunday.
It refers to a construction made by Vīrayya, son of Bhīmuḍu Ammana.

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. B 210 of 1936.)
GANḌIGANAMALA, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Base of a sculpture in a shrine near the ruined Śiva temple.

This record is dated in the cyclic year Manmatha, Śrāvaṇa śu. 15. It refers to a
certain Āvumma, wife of Vēlvula Basvireḍi, who became pēraṅṭālu (i.e., committed
sati).

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. B 261 of 1936.)
PEDDA-CHINTAKUMṬA, (NEAR ĀḶḶAGAḌḌA), SIRUVEL TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Sāṁbaśivēśvara temple.
This inscription is dated in the cyclic year Hemaḷaṁbi, Āshāḍha, ba. 7.

It states that Tammuḷa paruvatana got on image of Gaṇēśa made by Ka[nu]mala
Kommana and granted (to him) some land near Yammanūru. It also refers to the
grant of a pamnasa by the Kaṁsala community.

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. B 231 of 1938.)
EṞṞAGUḌIPĀḌU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the prākāra of the Chennakēśavasvāmi temple.

It is dated in cyclic year Viḷaṁbi, Māgha, ba. 10 and the characters of the
inscription can be assigned to 16th century.

Records the obeisance (sēva) of Mumaḍi Reḍḍi, son of Yanugu (Yēnugu) Māchi
Reḍi to the god Chennakēśavamurti of Eṟṟaguḍi Nāḍu.

l.te: śrīmatē rāmānujāyanamaḥ [||*] viḷaṁbi saṁvatsara māgha ba 10 lu
reḍi koḍuku mumaḍireḍi śē(sē)·i(yi)ṁccina śā(sē)pa | maṁgaḷamahā

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. B 273 of 1936.)
INJĒḌU, KOILKUNTLA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab built into the wall of a room on the north side of the central platform
inside the prākāra of the Venkaṭēśvara temple.
This record is dated in the cyclic year, Śubhakṛitu, Vaiśākha śu. 5.

Registers the gift of lands containing the Tamarind trees and rēgaḍu lands for
maintaining the worship and offerings to god Venkaṭāḍri of Iñjēḍu by Maṭli
Varadarāju.

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. B 222 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the base of a broken image of the Āñjanēya on the roadside about half
kilometer to the east of the village.
It is dated in the cyclic year, Krōdhi, Bhādrapada, ba. 5, Wednesday.

It records the consecration of the god Hanumantha sculptured by certain Gānula
Nārema.

inscription.head:
No. 266.
(A.R. No. B 50 of 1938.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TIRUCHIRAPALLI TRICHINAPULY TALUK AND
DISTRICT, TAMIL NADU.
inscription.p:
On the west wall of the Śankarālaya in the fourth prākāra of the
Jaṁbukēśvara temple.

This undated inscription can be assigned to the 17th century on palaeographic
grounds.

This incomplete inscription mention śrīman Mahādēva Makhi prabhu, who is
described as Sadāchāra and Kaḷasamagāmadhīra.

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. B 209 of 1936.)
GANḌIGANAMALA, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up near a well called Mālabhāvi, about 1/4 km to the
north-east of the village.
It is dated in the cyclic year, Parābhava, Āshāḍha, śu. 11.

It refers to the digging of two wells in the village Gaṇḍḍiganumulu by Rāja
Timmā reḍḍi for the merit of his parents Basivireḍi and Aṁkamasāni. The inscription
was engraved by Pedamallayya of Sathanapalli.

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. B 224 of 1938.)
DONḌAPĀḌU (Hamlet of Valasapalle), KAMALAPURAM TALUK,
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying near the Gōpālasvāmai temple.
This inscription is dated in the cyclic year, Kīlaka, Vaiśāka, śu. 11.

States that Vōbulreḍḍi, Naḷavōbulrēḍḍi, Papavōbulreḍḍi, Pedakoṁḍreḍḍi, the five
sons of Siṅgamareḍḍi Timmāreḍḍi and Bhūmāsani constructed the well in the garden
of Malināyaka and made a endowment of land as dasavandam for the merit of
their parents.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. B 328 of 1936.)
URUṬŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A slab set up in Survey No. 1/2, about 1(1/2) miles to the east of the village.

This undated inscription can be assigned to 18th century on the basis of
palaeography.

States that the pillar (kaṁbhaṁ) demarcates the boundry between the three
villages Kirttipalle alias Timmarājapuram, Uruṭūru and Peddanapāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(A.R. No. B 309 of 1938.)
MAILAVARAM, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar set up in the Śiva temple in the village.
It is dated in the cyclic year Virōdhikṛit Vaiśs6kha, śu. 5 Sunday.

Records the construction of bhōgamaṇḍapa for the god Sanita Mallaya and
grand of land by the mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kammarāju Rāmayaīṅgāru for the merit
of Dēvarāya Mahārāya and Hannayamma and Siddhayamma.

inscription.head:
No. 271.
(A.R. No. B 225 of 1936.)
MĀNNĒPALLEPADU, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near a well.
It is dated in the cyclic year Pramāi Jyēshṭha ba. 10.
It states that a certain Kōlla, son of Dēvaṁ Mutaya excavated the well.

inscription.head:
No. 272.
(A.R. No. B 329 of 1938.)
PRĀTAKŌṬA, NANDIKOTKURU TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the compound of the Vīrabhadrasvāmi temple.
This record is dated in the cyclic year Ānanda, Śrāvana, ba. 2.

Damaged. Seems to record the gifts of land to the temple of Vīrabhadrasvāmi
for maintaining perpetual lamps.

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(A.R. No. B 268 of 1938.)
PEDDACHAPPALI, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the temple of the Agastyēśvara svāmi temple.
It is dated cyclic year Ānanda, Bhādrapada, śu. 5.

States that certain Mahānandaya, son of Vellamāri Timmarāju offered his
salutations (daṁḍālu) to the god Hara i.e., Śiva.

l.te: ·ānaṁda saṁvatsara bhādrapada śu 5 lu haruniki vella

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(A.R. No. B 328 of 1938.)
PRĀTAKŌṬA, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the Maṇḍapa of Chennakēśava temple.

This inscription is dated in the cyclic year Rākshasa, Vaiśākha, śu. 2 and may be
corresponded to the 17th century.

Registers an agreement (Yedureḍa) executed by Kōnapa, son of Sthānāpati
Anantarasa to Magatala Timmaya and other reḍḍis and Karaṇams of the village
Prātakōṭa for 30 varahās and remitted the pulavari tax.

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. B 245 of 1936.)
KUMUDAVALLI, BHIMAVARAM TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar lying in front of the Viśvēśvara temple.
It is dated in cyclic year Kāḷayukti, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 4 Sunday.

Slightly damaged, Refers to the walk by Ārādhyula Ammarāju and Kumudavelli
Lingaya to fix the boundries from the village Nūrālamkōṭa.

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. B 197 of 1937.)
VILLIYAṈŪR, PONDICHERY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the maṇḍapa of the central shrine in the Tirukkāmīśvara temple.
It is dated in the cyclic year Kāḷayaukti, Kārthika, ashṭami.
Records the perpetual devotion of a certain Padmanabhayya.

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. B 69 of 1938.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TIRUCHINOPOLY TALUK, TIRUCHINOPOLY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Śankarālaya in the fourth prākāra Jaṁbukēśvara temple.
This inscription can be assigned to 17th century on the basis of palaeography.
Refers to the death of the wife of Mahādēva Makhīṁdra (Sadāśiva Makhīṁdra) ?

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. B 262 of 1936.)
PEDDA CHINTAKUMṬA (NEAR ĀḶḶAGAḌḌA), SIRUVEL TALUK,
KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the pillar in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Sāmbaśiva temple.
This inscription is dated in the cyclic year Kshaya, Kārttika, ba. 13, Monday.

States that Malli-seṭṭī, son of Gānula[va]jiseṭṭī of Sirangipuram constructed a
pond by raising dam across a stream flowing through the field of Chīñtakuṇta. This
record was engraved by Pāpayya, son of kāraṇam Tipparāju Nāgayya.

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. B 345 of 1937.)
MĀḌUGULA, PALNADU TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On one of the pillar (sixth pillar) in the Kāḷēśvara temple.
Paleographically the record can be assigned to 6th century A.D.
It reads: Siṁgadhamana.

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. B 342 of 1937.)
MĀḌUGULA, PALNADU TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a fourth pillar set up in the compound of the Kāḷēśvara temple.

This label inscription can be assigned to the 7th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography.
It reads: Vi[ku]ra saṁghāṭa.

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. B 344 of 1937.)
MĀḌUGULA, PALNADU TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On sixth pillar set up in the compound of the Kāḷēśvara temple.

This label inscription can be assigned to the 7th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography.

It reads: 1. Chirtāḷmaṇiṛ Śrī
2. Chūṟ śovva śrī

Probably refers to a sculptor who is described as expert in Chisling (the stone)
[chūṟu-sovva].

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. B 346 of 1937.)
MĀḌUGULA, PALNADU TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Front wall of the small shrine in the courtyard of the Kāḷēśvara temple.

This label inscription can be assigned to the 7th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography.
It reads: Śrī Ānākarttar.
Probably refers to a senior sculptor.

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. B 347 of 1937.)
MĀḌUGULA, PALNADU TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the nandi-maṇḍapa of the Śiva shrine.

This label inscription can be assigned to the 7th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography.

It probably refers to the lamp (dīpambu) set up by Saṁsārabhīta.
(cf. G.E. No. 38 of 1939.)

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. B 339 of 1937.)
MĀḌUGULA, PALNADU TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: The fourth pillar in the compound of the Kāḷēśvara temple.

This label inscription can be assigned to the 9th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography.
It reads: Pāṭiyara Kaṟavu Aḷḷi durjjaya.

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. B 354 of 1938.)
UNḌAVALLI, MANGALAGIRI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Lower facade of the three-storeyed cave.
This can be assigned to 7th-8th centuries A.D. on the basis of palaeography.
Reads: Śrī Ma[t] Pṟithvi.

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. B 355 of 1938.)
UNḌAVALLI, MANGALAGIRI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the lower facade of the three-storeyed cave.

The badly damaged inscription can be assigned to 7th-8th centuries A.D. on the
basis of palaeography.
Reads: Śrī Pṟithvi.

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. B 298 of 1936.)
ANANTĀPURAM, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the ruines of the Śiva temple near the village.

This undated inscription may be assigned to 8th century on the basis of
palaeography.

It records the death of a hero Koṟjrṉṟu the son (maganṟu) of Ōbaḷi Muduḷa in
a cattle field.

l.note:
A figure of a hero with a bow in
the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. B 299 of 1936.)
ANANTĀPURAM, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: The second slab set-up in the Survey No. 135 on the ruins of the Śiva temple.
The inscription may be assigned to the 8th century on the basis of palaeography.
Badly damaged and wornout. It mentions sāśana (inscription) and dēgula (temple).

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(A.R. No. B 299 of 1938.)
VELDURTI, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying near the Śiva temple.

This undated record may be assigned to the 9th century of the basis of
palaeography.

Incomplete. Seems to refers to death (aṇicheḷa) of Nivāṟarāḍu the son of certain
Kalligōlor Sindarāju and Ugrapottuja.

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. B 336 of 1937.)
GURAZĀLA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the Kōṭasāni well.
This undated inscription can be assigned palaeography to 9th century.

States that a certain Ḻumaiya, the son of Jābinnal Kaḷahabōya of Paḷḷinādu and
also another warrior (magaṇṟu) called Ugra attacked and killed Jayama, while
singing Eṟumāṭī gītamulu in the cattle raid at battle of Vēsaramu.

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(A.R. No. B 331 of 1936.)
HANUMALAGUTTI, PRODATTUR TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A pillar of the nandi-maṇḍapa in front of the Śiva temple.
This label inscription may be assigned to 10th century on the basis of palaeography.

It reads Svasti Śrīchōḷara parvatarishi chitaya i.e. the funaral pyre of Śrī Chōḷara
parvatarishi.

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(A.R. No. B 334 of 1937.)
GURZĀLA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On hero-stone lying on the left side of the Gurzālamma temple.
It can be assigned to 9th century A.D. on palaeographical grounds.

Damaged and mutilated and mention the territorial division. Anyāvaramu and
Pallinādu.

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. B 332 of 1936.)
HANUMALAGUTI, PRODATTUR TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On hero-stone set up against the dhvajastaṁbha in front of the Śiva temple.

This undated inscription can be assigned to 9th century A.D. on palaeographical
grounds.
It mentions a certain Irugamabadirāju, son of Chavukabaṇṭu.

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(A.R. No. B 331 of 1937.)
AYYANGĀRI PĀLEM (NEAR PULIPĀḌU), PALNAD TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the maṇḍapa of the ruined Bobbanāgi temple.
This inscription can be assigned to 9th century A.D. on the basis of palaeography.

It states that the temple of Jalpēśa which is incomparable, (anupam-atiśayam)
pre-eminent (abhinutamatim), graceful (lalita), inconceivable (achintyam), immovable
(achalam) and beyond all blames (anindyam) was built by an architect Maindarāma,
who belongs to the family of ācharyya Kalgarābharaṇāchāryya, an expert (karmmajña),
probably in making the sculptures, belongs to the fourth caste.

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(A.R. No. B 332 of 1937.)
AYYANGĀRI PĀLEM (NEAR PULIPĀḌU), PALNAD TALUK,
GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the temple of Bobbanāgi.

This inscription can be assigned to 9th century A.D. on the basis of palaeography
and other internal evidence.

Mentions Chāmōya, a pupil of Kālgarābharaṇa, who is described as an ornament
of the Viśvakarma kula and the āchāryya of Jalpēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. B 333 of 1937.)
AYYANGĀRI PĀLEM, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the ardhamaṇḍapa of the ruined Bobbanāgi temple.

This label inscription can be assigned to 9th century A.D. on palaeographical
grounds.
It mentions Śrī Kālgarbhāra[ṇa].

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. B 220 of 1938.)
KOṆḌAMUṬLA, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up to the east of the ruined maṇḍapa in Survey No. 395
to the east of the village.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 10th century A.D. on the basis
of palaeography.

Slightly damaged. Registers the gift of an agrahāra namely Immaḍi dēvāgrahāra
by Kumāra Kēsiya rāju to certain Bhīmasinga bhaṭṭāra.

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. B 213 of 1938.)
CHIḌPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in front of the Kālabhairavasvāmin shrine in the
Agastyēśvarasvāmin temple.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 10th century A.D. on the basis
of palaeography.

Fragmentary and begining lost. Registers the gift of 10 maruturu of dryland and
a puṭṭī of wet land (vaḍla paṭṭu) with the exemption of taxes. It is stated that the
gift is said to be protected by the Cheppali 1000 community.

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. B 212 of 1938.)
CHIḌPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the Ānjanēyasvāmi temple.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 10th-11th century on the basis of
palaeography.

Damaged and portion of the text written in Telugu verse. Registers, the gift of
50 maruturs of land by certain Namena peggaḍa got renovated the temple, probably
erected by Agasti (Agasthya). It also records on the second side to the value of
Dharma and eulogises the charity of Ammenapeggaḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. B 234 of 1936.)
RĀVULĀPURAM, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up near a well in survey No. 230 by the side of the cart-track leading
to Rāvulāpuram from Manēpallepādu.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 11th century A.D. on palaeographical
grounds.

It records the gift (dharmmuvu) of land of 1 kha in extent to the God
Bhīmēśvara for the merit of Chōḍarāju.

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. B 287 of 1936.)
KUṄKANŪRU, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Hero-stone setup outside the compound of the Rāmaliṅgēśvara temple situated on
the east side of the village.

This undated record may be assigned to the 12th century on the basis of
palaeography.
It mentions a certain Sōma-gāvuḍa, the devotee of Rāmēśvaradēva.

edition.h4: TEXT. A figure of a hero in fighting posture is sculptured.

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. B 218 of 1936.)
KANAMALAPŪḌI, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the back side pillar in Survey No. 20.

This undated epigraphy may be assigned to the 12th century on the basis of
palaeography.

Peeled off at the end. It seems to record the consecration of some deity by
Katyadēsati, in the village Kadamiṭlapūmḍi which belonges to the god Śrīkaṁṭhīśvara
of Kaṁcheṟvula.

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(A.R. No. B 239 of 1936.)
ŚĀVAYĀLPURAM, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar lying by the road side in the village.
This undated record may be assigned to the 12th century A.D. on palaeography grounds.
It mentions king, Purōhita, Daṇḍanāyaka, Sēnāpati, Karaṇamu and Bōya as witnesses.
Damaged and begining lost. Purport is not clear. Seems to refers to setting up of a pillar.

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(A.R. No. B 231 of 1936.)
POṬLŪRU, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the ruined Vishṇu Temple situated to the west of the village.

This inscription, the date portion of which is lost, may be assigned to 13th
century A.D. on the basis of palaeography.

Begining portion of the epigraph is broken and lost. It seems to record a gift of
a plate (paḷḷeraṁ) by a certain Garuḍa-dāsi.

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(A.R. No. B 296 of 1937.)
BHAṬṬIPRŌLU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the kalyānamaṇḍapa in the Viṭṭhalēśvara temple.

This incomplete record is dated in the Cyclic year Āngīrasa, Chaitra, ba. 1,
Wednesday. The record can be assigned to 13th century A.D. on palaeographical
grounds.
It mentions a tax collector (sunkādhikāri) Pōnkala Mallayya Gōvinda Peggaḍa.

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(A.R. No. B 353 of 1937.)
BALLIPAṞṞU, GUDIVADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Pillar built into the threshold of the entrance into the compound of the
Vēnugōpālasvāmi temple.

This undated inscription can be assigned to the 13th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography and internal evidences.

Much damaged and worn out. Describes a chief belonging to Ḻonkula family
and attributed with a string of epithets such as Sōmakula kamalendu holding
Kapidhvaja. It registers a gift of land, cow (mōdālu) for maintaining a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Mallikārjuna. The gift is stated to have been entrusted to
certain Appanabōya for its maintenance.

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(A.R. No. B 324 of 1937.)
PEDAPULIVAṞṞU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar in the temple of Narēndrēśvarasvami.
This undated record can be assigned to 14th century on the basis of palaeography.

It records the construction of the temple to Narēndrēśvara linga and maintain
the daily offerings for the merit of his mother Nāgamma and sister Pinnanāgamma
respectively by Sarviseṭṭi. The donor Sarvisetti is described as the son of Jaluḍu
Kaṁniseṭṭi.

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(A.R. No. B 307 of 1936.)
POṬṬIPĀḌU, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: The Garuḍastambha set up in front of the Narasimhasvāmi temple.

This undated record may be assigned to the 14th century on the basis of
palaeography.
It records that certain Virābhatu installed the pillar (kaṁbhaṁ)

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(A.R. No. B 319 of 1937.)
PEDDAPALLE, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a sculptured stone lying near the survey No. 59 to the west of the village.The sculpture shows the figure of a man with a dagger in his right-hand and a bow in the
hand.

This undated inscription can be assigned to 14th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography.
It reads: konḍaya pratima (the sculpture of Konḍaya.)

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(A.R. No. B 211 of 1936.)
GARIKAPĀḌU, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
An image lying under baniyan tree in survey No. 111/A, to the east of the
village.

This undated inscription may be assigned to 14th century A.D. on palaeography
grounds.

It mentions certain Mōli Tikkanāyakuḍu alias Periya Perumāladāsu. The image
contains figure of a person with folded hands probably refers to the person
mentioned in the epigraph.

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(A.R. No. B 320 of 1937.)
PEDDAPALLE, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the base of an image of Durga lying in the Palmyre grove near the
Karanams house.
This record can be assigned to 15th century A.D. on the basis of palaeography.

Refers to the consecration of the image of Māramma by a certain Binḍḍi Simgu.
The inscription is engraved by Pōtāchāri.

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(A.R. No. B 204 of 1937.)
VILLIYANUR, VILLUPURAM TALUK, PONDICHERRY DISTRICT,
PONDICHERRY.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Yāgaśāla of the Tirukkāmeśvara temple.
This epigraph can be assigned to 15th century A.D. on the basis of palaeography.

It records the offerings to the God Tirumāmēśvara by Tillenāyukuḍu, son of
minister (arasappa) Eṟṟama of Konḍepi.

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(A.R. No. B 240 of 1938.)
KAMALĀPURAM, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the cart track between Kamalāpuram and Peddachappalli.

This undated inscription can be assigned to 15th century A.D. on the basis of both
internal and palaeographical grounds.

Specifies the boundary between the villages Peddachappalle alias Kṛishṇārāyapuram.
Chinna-Chappalle and Kamalāpuram. It bears carving of Vāmana-figure.

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(A.R. No. B 229 of 1938.)
EṞṞAGUḌIPĀḌU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the inner prākāra of Chennakēśavasvāmi temple.
This inscription can be assigned to 15th century A.D. on the basis of palaeography.
Incomplete. It records the construction of the temple of Vināyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(A.R. No. B 314 of 1936.)
INDUKŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of a slab set up in front of the Mugguru Ankkālamma temple.
This inscription can be assigned to 15th century on the basis of palaeography.

Broken and mutilated. Refers to koul, probably of a land, to be maintained by
certain persons inspite of holding the offices of Reḍḍi, Karanam Pārupatyam and
chief.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(A.R. No. B 329 of 1936.)
URUṬŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab lying near the Gaṛēśa shrine to the north of the village.

This undated inscription can be assigned to 15th century on the basis of
palaeography.

Begining lost. Registers a grant of income drived from taxes on the kāmpus at
the rate of 12 māḍas per year, made to a temple by Dommari community, for the
worship of the god on different occasions like śivarātri for the merit of twenty four
communities.

l.te: nu dhāravōśi yistimigāna mīruṁ grāmamu mahāja-
lōnu vadhīṁccina dōṣāna bōvuvāru gōvu brāhmānini vāraṇāśi

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(A.R. No. B 236 of 1936.)
RĒMIḌICHERLA, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Lintel of the doorway of the garbhagṛiha of the Vishṇu temple.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 15th century A.D. on palaeography
grounds.

It records the Gumṭala Singāreḍḍi caused the carving (varuvuṁbani) of the
doorway of the temple of Kēśavanātha.

l.te: śrīkēśavanādhani vākiliguṁṭala siṁgāreḍi sēyiṁccina[varu] vuṁ bani

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(A.R. No. B 237 of 1936.)
RĒMIḌICHERLA, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Mutilated hero-stone lying on the compound of the Śiva temple.

This inscription may be assigned to the 16th century A.D. on palaeographcal
grounds.

Damaged. Purport is not clear. However, it refers to the setting up of the hero-
stone on which this inscription is engraved.

inscription.head:
No. 319.
(A.R. No. B 304 of 1938.)
BALAPANAGŪḌŪRU, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab set up in the Śiva temple.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 16th century A.D. on the basis
palaeography and internal evidence.

Broken and partly mutiḷated. It registers the gift of a flower garden entrusted
to Naṁbi Tipparāju, by Sōmalraju Timmayadēva maharāja for maintaining the
flower offerings to the god Perumālu situated at Bamḍibōyilaguḍuru.

It also records the construction of large well and picotta (ētāmu) in the center
of the garden.

l.te: nidi [|*] yī tōṁṭaku doravi drovvi yēṁta-
ṁ sēyiṁcuka bradikēdi yī tōṁṭaki rā-
ni ca[ṁ*]ppina pāpānaṁbōduru [||*] gaṁga karada kapi

inscription.head:
No. 320.
(A.R. No. B 291 of 1938.)
VALASAPALLE, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of stone lying near a well near the ‘burz.’

This undated inscription can be assigned to the 16th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography.

Fragmentary. Mentions Kōnēṭam[mma], daughter of Tiruma • • aḷavāru, and
Nṛisiṁhavarājaya, the village Bhōga agrahārāṁ, Doṇḍapā[ṭa*]Nṛisimha puram.

inscription.head:
No. 321.
(A.R. No. B 292 of 1938.)
VEḌUDŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar lying by the road side at the entrance of the village.
This can be assigned to the 16th century A.D. on the basis of palaeography.
Completely damaged. Records imprecatory verses only.

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(A.R. No. B 326 of 1938.)
PRĀTAKŌṬA, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On pedestal of an image of a saint kept in the temple of the
Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple.
Palaeographically, this can be assigned to 16th century A.D.

It refers to tapōdhana (ascetic) Mūrthidēva paṇḍita probably the devotee of
Nāganāthadēva of Nāraṇapuram.

inscription.head:
No. 323.
(A.R. No. B 203 of 1938.)
ANIMELA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying about a furlong to the south-west of the Saṇgamēśvara svāmi temple.

This undated inscription can be assigned to the 16th century on the basis of
palaeography.

Records the gift of lands at the rate of 50 varāhas to certain Timmapa with a
stipulation to play the Vīṇa and the Kinnara instruments in the temple of Sangamēśvara
at Pālem by him and his descendants.

inscription.head:
No. 324.
(A.R. No. B 208 of 1938.)
CHIḌIPIRĀLA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Northern wall of the kitchen in the Agastyēśvara svāmi temple.

This damaged and undated inscription may be assigned to the 16th century on
the basis of palaeography.

Fragmentary. It registers the gift of land to several brahmanas (names mentioned)
of different gōtras.

l.te: • • • • • śapatli basuvābhaṭlu koḍuku tiṁmayakupritti bakaṭi [|*] ka • •
• • pābhaṭla koḍku dēcayaku vritti | vōkaṭi [|*] haritasagōtra ·āpastaṁ

vriti 1 vokaṭi kāśipa gōtraṁ ·āpastaṁba sūtra yajaśā • • •

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(A.R. No. B 371 of 1937.)
VIṬHALAM, VAYALPADU TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On Gōpura slab of a ruined temple left side entrance.
This can be assigned to the 16th century A.D. on the basis of palaeography.

Damaged. Registers some gift of land probably to the temple. Mentions the
village Viṭṭhalam and Pungipalli sthalam.

l.te:
• • • • • paḍamara[ta]rugoṁḍuku rāku baṁḍalōnu gānula • • • vīrānaku rāmacaṁdrapuraṁ
polamēra vallagaṭṭu mēragānu • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(A.R. No. B 373 of 1937.)
VIṬHALAM, VAYALPADU TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the plinth of the Viṭhalēśvara temple situated in the village.
Damaged. Refers to a certain Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara whose name is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(A.R. No. B 361 of 1937.)
NAḌIGUḌḌA, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a rock called Chākalibaṇḍa about half a mile to the east of the village.
This label inscription can be assigned to the 16th century A.D.
It states that the stone grinder (rōlu) was made by certain Gaṁggi Pāpana.

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(A.R. No. B 310 of 1937.)
KŪCHINAPŪḌI, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the mukhamaṇḍapa of the Vīrēśvarasvāmin temple.
This undated record can be assigned to 16th century on the palaeographical grounds.

States that Chadaraṁgga Vīrayya constructed a maṭham called Nirāsimaṭham and
consecrated a linga called Gōkarṇṇa, probably in the maṭham. The prefix chada raṁgga to
the personal name Vīrayya indicates the proffession of chess game by the donor.

inscription.head:
No. 329.
(A.R. No. B 326 of 1937.)
PEDAPULIVARRU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the temple of the Narēndrēśvarasvāmi.
Palaeographically, the record can be assigned to 16th century A.D.

States that a certain Vuppala Pāṭi Nēḍu had donated an image of Nandi for the
god.

inscription.head:
No. 330.
(A.R. No. B 312 of 1936.)
ĒṬŪRU, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A slab in the Chennakēśava temple.

This damaged inscription can be dated to the 16th century on Palaeographical
grounds.

Mutilated and damaged. Seems to register some gifts of land in Ghaṇḍikōṭa-sīma
and also refers to the digging of canal by Kāmpus.

l.te: • • • • • nnu maḍinni kāṁ • • • kālva tavvana kāṁpulu ·a[ṁ]

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(A.R. No. B 326 of 1936.)
TALAPANŪR, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in front of the Āñjanēya temple.

This undated record may be assigned to the 16th century on the basis of
Palaeography.
Begining of the inscription is lost and only the imprecatory portion is available.

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. B 256 of 1936.)
ANANTAPALLE, TADEPALLIGUDEM TALUK, WEST GODAVARI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Pillar set up at the entrance into the mukhamaṇḍapa of the
Vēṇugōpalasvāmi temple.

This undated inscription can be assigned to 16th century on the basis of
palaeography.

Built in and begining lost. Seems to renew the grant of lands and grant of
further land as a sarvamānya śrōtriyam to one Kaṁkajiyya.

inscription.head:
No. 333.
(A.R. No. B 342 of 1936.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT, KARNATAKA.
inscription.p:
Pedestal of the Vishṇu image lying in the compound of the Viṭṭhalasvāmi
temple.
It may be dated to the 16th century on the basis of Palaeography.
It reads Śrī Mādhava mūrtti.

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(A.R. No. B 343 of 1936.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT. KARNATAKA.
inscription.p:
On a pedestal of another Vishṇu image lying in the compound of the
Viṭṭhalasvāmi temple.
Palaeographically, this can be assigned to 16th century A.D.
It reads Śrī Mādhava mūrtti.

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(A.R. No. B 343 of 1936.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT, KARNATAKA.
inscription.p: On a pedestal of the Vishṇu image kept in the compound of Viṭṭhalasvāmi temple.
Palaeographically, this can be assigned to 16th century A.D.
It reads Vīpa Kēśavamūrthi.

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(A.R. No. B 247 of 1938.)
KOTTAPALLE, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A broken slab lying the shrine of Rāma in the village.

This undated inscription can be assigned to the 16th century on the basis of
palaeography.

Broken. Records the gift of lands at Paḍadurti and renovation of certain canals
and demarcated with boundaries stones containing Vāmanamudras and given to
certain Timmareḍi by Gangireḍi and • • • • • dēva mahārāja.

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(A.R. No. B 324 of 1936.)
PĀLAGIRI, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in front of the Śiva temple.

This undated record can be assigned to the 16th century on palaeography
grounds.

Registers the gift of three rūkas in the from of taxes on marriages among the
Balija śeṭṭis to donate the same to temple of Bhīmēśvara probably in presence of
mahānāḍu (assembly).

inscription.head:
No. 338.
(A.R. No. B 194 of 1938.)
ANIMELA, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragmentary slab lying near the stream.

This undated record may be assigned to the 16th century on the basis of
palaeography.
Fragmentary and incomplete. It records only the imprecatory portions.

inscription.head:
No. 339.
(A.R. No. B 107 of 1938.)
ŚRĪ RANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK, AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: V prākāra south wall of the Garuḍa-maṇḍapa of the Śrī Ranganatha temple.
This undated record may be assigned to the 16th century on internal evidence.

The records refers to names of the ladies viz., Nāchāramma, wife of Savaram
Veṅkaṭappa nāyaka, Vōbulamma, wife of Komara Venkaṭappa nāyak, Chī(Sī)tamma,
(wife) of Raghunātha-nāyaka and Venkaṭamma (wife) of Veṅkaṭappa nāyaka.
(Published is South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XXIV, Ins No. 587, p 558.)

inscription.head:
No. 340.
(A.R. No. B 312 of 1938.)
PEDDAṆḌLŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the platform infront of the Kēśava temple.

This undated record may be assigned to the 17th century on the basis of
palaeography.

Registers a gift of land measuring five munt tas (specified) made for the
construction of a śikhara.

inscription.head:
No. 341.
(A.R. No. B 268 of 1936.)
DĒVARĀYAPURAM, SIRUVEL TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Mutilated slab kept in the shrine of Āñjanēya.

The mutilated and undated inscription can be assigned to 17th century on the
basis of palaeography.
Mention the god Ahōbalēśvara and Hanumantha at Chinttakuṇta.

edition.h4: TEXT. The Figure of a crescent, a Linga and the Sun are carred at the top of

inscription.head:
No. 342.
(A.R. No. B 233 of 1936.)
POṬḶŪRU, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pedestal of an image lying in the compound of the Vishṇu temple.
This undated epigraph may be assigned to 17th century on palaeographic grounds.
It contain the name Periyālvāru probably the image on the pedestal.

inscription.head:
No. 343.
(A.R. No. B 312 of 1937.)
NALLŪRU, REPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab built into the celing of the Lakshmīnarasimhasvāmi temple.
This undated inscription can be assigned to 17th century A.D.

A verse in Kanda metre from Rāmaliṅgēśvara śatakaṁ and states that those
who do not perform any dāna and dhārma cannot live in this earth happily.

l.te: nānāvithamulaṁ bratukulēmā nitasujanāttuṁgga mādala liṁgga || śrīrāmaliṁgēśvarā |

inscription.head:
No. 344.
(A.R. No. B 283 of 1936.)
VIPANAGANḌLA NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A slab lying in Survey No. 291, about (1/2) Kilometer to the north wall of the village.

This undated inscription can be assigned to 17th century on the basis of the
palaeography.

The damaged and incomplete inscription seems to record the gift of a village
and lands for the merit of some individual. It also mentions the village Vipunaganḍla.

edition.h4:
TEXT. A figure of Vāmana is carved in sitting posture with a kamaṇḍala in
hand respectively.

inscription.head:
No. 345.
(A.R. No. B 230 of 1936.)
PĒRŪRPĀḌU, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in Survey No. 392/2, by the side of the east track leading to
Koppukoṇḍa village.

This undated record may be assigned to 17th century on the basis of the
palaeography.

It states that this pillar obviously the one bearing inscription is a boundary pillar
between the village Vemṭarāya and Doṇḍapāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 346.
(A.R. No. B 232 of 1936.)
POṬLŪRU, VINUKONDA TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pedestal of a mutilated lying in the compound of the Vishṇu temple.

This undated record may be assigned to 17th century A.D. on the basis of the
palaeography.

It contains the name Kulaśēkharaḍu which means that the image is that of
Kulaśēkhara·ālvār.

inscription.head:
No. 347.
(A.R. No. B 316 of 1936.)
KŌLAVALI, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Fragment of a slab lying by the side of a cart track about a kilometer to the
east of the village.

This undated inscription may be assigned to 17th century on the basis of the
palaeography.

Fragmentary. Seems to register the gift of a vṛitti and flower garden to the
temple of Chennakēśavadēva on the occasion of the consecration of the god therein.

inscription.head:
No. 348.
(A.R. No. B 338 of 1936.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT, KARNATAKA.
inscription.p: Pedestal of the Vishṇu image kept in the Travellers Bungalow.
This inscription can be assigned to 17th century on the basis of the palaeography.

Mentions certain Kochchya, son of Bhīmayya and grandson of Bhīmayya who
was probably the sculptor of the image.

inscription.head:
No. 349.
(A.R. No. B 338 of 1936.)
SAMBAṬŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a cornice-stone of the bali-pīṭha in the Chennakēśava temple.

This undated inscription can be assigned to 17th century A.D. on the basis of the
palaeography.

Badly damaged and wornout. A son of (name not clear) somebody had caused
to construct the bali-pīṭha.

inscription.head:
No. 350.
(A.R. No. B 118 of 1936.)
ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: V prākāra slab in the tank near the Vasanta maṇḍapa.
This undated damaged record is assigned to 17th century A.D.
It refers the donation made by Appalammagāru, the queen (ḍēuvulu) of Rāmarājaya.
(Published in South Indian Inscription Vol. XXIV, Ins. No. 590, p. 559.)

inscription.head:
No. 351.
(A.R. No. B 260 of 1938.)
PĀYASAMPALLE, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the village.

This undated inscription can be assigned to the 18th century on the basis of
palaeography.

It records the prayer of Ghaṭākarnā for the protection against hoof diseases
(Khurarōga) in cattle in the city of Viraṭa and mentions Sahadēva's name.

inscription.head:
No. 352.
(A.R. No. B 302 of 1938.)
VENKAṬĀPURAM, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the village.

This undated inscription may be assigned to the 18th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography.

Records an invocation of Ghaṇṭākarna for warding of the disease of cattle and
refers to Sahadēva, also known as Tantrīpāla, one of the Pāṇḍavas who treats the
cows of Virāṭapaṭṭaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 353.
(A.R. No. B 17 of 1938.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a pillar in the south wall of Utsava maṇḍapa (inside) of the first prākāra of
the Jaṁbukēśvara temple.
This record is engraved in 18th century characters.

Refers to certain of jayastamba containing three names of god Śiva by
Sadāśiva-Dīkshita.

inscription.head:
No. 354.
(A.R. No. B 43 of 1938.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Loose stone lying in the third prākāra of the Jaṁbukēśvara temple.
This inscription is dated on the basis of palaeography to the 18th century.
It reads Tāchi-Nāchyārakka.

inscription.head:
No. 355.
(A.R. No. B 54 of 1938.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Śankarālaya in the fourth prākāra.
This inscription can be assigned to the 18th century on the basis of palaeography.

Records an incomplete verse in praise of Sadāśivamakhimdra and his literary
attainments in all languages.

l.te: bha • sakalabhāṣalaṁdu kavitalu racanalu śēyunaṭṭi dhāralu • • laya •

inscription.head:
No. 356.
(A.R. No. B 61 of 1938.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Śankarālaya in the fourth prākāra.

Contains some of the laudatory verses on God Śiva composed by Sadāśiva
Makhīndra.

inscription.head:
No. 357.
(A.R. No. B 62 of 1938.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT, TAMILNADU.
inscription.p:
On the south wall of the Śankarālaya in the fourth prākāra of the
Jambukēśvara temple.

This undated inscription can be assigned to the 18th century on the basis of
palaeography.
It refers to a saint Sadāśiva Makhi and of the Śiva cult which be propogated.

inscription.head:
No. 358.
(A.R. No. B 62 of 1938.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the verandah of the second prākāra of the Jambukēśvara temple.
Palaeographically this can be assigned to 18th century.
Refers to three names of god Śiva and praise of Sadāśiva makhēndra.

inscription.head:
No. 359.
(A.R. No. B 127 of 1937.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT, TAMILNADU.
inscription.p: On the wall of the east gōpura leading to the third prākāra.

This record can be assigned to the late 18th century A.D. on palaeographical
grounds.
States that the adherents of Advaita philosophy attain.

inscription.head:
No. 360.
(A.R. No. B 132 of 1937.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT, TAMILNADU.
inscription.p: On the outside south wall of the fourth prākāra left of entrance.

States that Sadāśiva Dīkshita planted the pillars engraved with Śivanāmatraya
called Śivanāma traya jaya stambham.

inscription.head:
No. 361.
(A.R. No. B 112 of 1937.)
JAṀBUKĒŚVARAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in the verandah of the second prākāra of the Jambukēśvara temple.
States that the channel is named as Ayyagaḷayya.

inscription.head:
No. 362.
(A.R. No. B 261 of 1938.)
PEDDANAPĀḌU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the boulder in a field about a furlong to the east of the ruined temples.

This mutilated and badly damaged inscription can be assigned to 18th century on
the basis of palaeography.
Mentions Śrīmatu and seems to record a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 363.
(A.R. No. B 280 of 1936.)
VIPANAGANḌLA, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
A fragment of a slab spread on the platform under the margōsa tree near the
well called Bommanabhāvi.

This undated inscription can be assigned to 18th century on the basis of
palaeography.

Incomplete and registers contributions levied on the produce of grain in the
village. It also mentions certain Venkaṭādhirāja.

inscription.head:
No. 364.
(A.R. No. B 369 of 1937.)
VĀYALPĀḌU, VAYALPADU TALUK CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab set up in front of the gōpura (proper right side) of the
Paṭṭābhirāmasvāmi temple.

This label inscription can be assigned to 18th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography.
Records prasing efficacy of Rāma-smaraṇa.

l.te: mādarēṇa bhaktyā sadāsmaraka mukti mupaitu jaṁtuḥ

inscription.head:
No. 365.
(A.R. No. B 215 of 1936.)
INUMEḶḶA, VINUKONDA TALUK GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the second face of the slab set up in front of the Bhāvanārāyṇa swāmy temple.

This undated record can be assigned to the 19th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeographical and internal evidences.

Registers the plantation of mango grove and a well on the north-west side of the
temple and donated these as gifts to the god by the Rāja Malrāju Ramaṇammagāru
in the name of the elder daughter Jūpalli Lakshmikāntamma who was the wife of
Rāja Malrāju Venkaṭa Punalā Rayanimgaru and the mother of Venkaṭa ramaṇayya
and Lakshmayya.

inscription.head:
No. 366.
(A.R. No. B 370 of 1937.)
VĀYALPĀḌU, VAYALPADU TALUK CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On one of the tiers of the gōpura of the Paṭṭābhirāmasvāmi temple.

This undated inscription can be assigned to the 19th century A.D. on the basis of
palaeography.
Damaged. Mentions the lands namely Ādalūri-guṁta and Āchama-guṁtta.

inscription.head:
No. 367.
(A.R. No. B 270 of 1938.)
RĀJUPĀLEM (Near Animela), KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab to the south side of the Śiva temple near the river.
Mentions the distance of the Kumdūru channel as 47 bāras of vilu (bow).
